available).\n66\t    pub usage: Option<TokenUsage>,\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\timpl ProviderResponse {\n70\t    /// Create a new basic response.\n71\t    pub fn new(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n72\t        Self {\n73\t            text: text.into(),\n74\t            tool_calls: vec![],\n75\t            duration_ms: 0,\n76\t            cost_usd: None,\n77\t            finish_reason: FinishReason::Stop,\n78\t            usage: None,\n79\t        }\n80\t    }\n81\t\n82\t    /// Add tool calls to the response.\n83\t    pub fn with_tool_calls(mut self, calls: Vec<ToolCall>) -> Self {\n84\t        self.tool_calls = calls;\n85\t        self.finish_reason = if !self.tool_calls.is_empty() {\n86\t            FinishReason::ToolCall\n87\t        } else {\n88\t            FinishReason::Stop\n89\t        };\n90\t        self\n91\t    }\n92\t\n93\t    /// Set the finish reason.\n94\t    pub fn with_finish_reason(mut self, reason: FinishReason) -> Self {\n95\t        self.finish_reason = reason;\n96\t        self\n97\t    }\n98\t\n99\t    /// Set the token usage.\n100\t    pub fn with_usage(mut self, usage: TokenUsage) -> Self {\n101\t        self.usage = Some(usage);\n102\t        self\n103\t    }\n104\t\n105\t    /// Set the duration.\n106\t    pub fn with_duration(mut self, duration_ms: u64) -> Self {\n107\t        self.duration_ms = duration_ms;\n108\t        self\n109\t    }\n110\t\n111\t    /// Set the cost.\n112\t    pub fn with_cost(mut self, cost: f64) -> Self {\n113\t        self.cost_usd = Some(cost);\n114\t        self\n115\t    }\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Reason why the provider's response ended.\n119\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\n120\tpub enum FinishReason {\n121\t    /// Normal stop sequence.\n122\t    #[default]\n123\t    Stop,\n124\t    /// Tool calls were made.\n125\t    ToolCall,\n126\t    /// Max tokens reached.\n127\t    MaxTokens,\n128\t    /// Error occurred.\n129\t    Error(String),\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\t/// Token usage information for a provider response.\n133\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n134\tpub struct TokenUsage {\n135\t    /// Input tokens consumed.\n136\t    pub input_tokens: u32,\n137\t\n138\t    /// Output tokens consumed.\n139\t    pub output_tokens: u32,\n140\t\n141\t    /// Cache read tokens (prompt caching hits).\n142\t    pub cache_read_tokens: u32,\n143\t\n144\t    /// Cache creation tokens (new cache entries).\n145\t    pub cache_creation_tokens: u32,\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\timpl TokenUsage {\n149\t    /// Total tokens consumed (input + output).\n150\t    pub fn total_tokens(&self) -> u64 {\n151\t        self.input_tokens as u64 + self.output_tokens as u64\n152\t    }\n153\t\n154\t    /// Total cache tokens (read + creation).\n155\t    pub fn total_cache_tokens(&self) -> u64 {\n156\t        self.cache_read_tokens as u64 + self.cache_creation_tokens as u64\n157\t    }\n158\t\n159\t    /// Create zero usage.\n160\t    pub fn zero() -> Self {\n161\t        Self {\n162\t            input_tokens: 0,\n163\t            output_tokens: 0,\n164\t            cache_read_tokens: 0,\n165\t            cache_creation_tokens: 0,\n166\t        }\n167\t    }\n168\t}\n169\t\n170\timpl Default for TokenUsage {\n171\t    fn default() -> Self {\n172\t        Self::zero()\n173\t    }\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t/// Tool definition passed to providers.\n177\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n178\tpub struct ProviderTool {\n179\t    /// Tool name.\n180\t    pub name: String,\n181\t\n182\t    /// Tool description.\n183\t    pub description: String,\n184\t\n185\t    /// Input schema (JSON Schema).\n186\t    pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,\n187\t}\n188\t\n189\timpl From<ToolDefinition> for ProviderTool {\n190\t    fn from(tool: ToolDefinition) -> Self {\n191\t        Self {\n192\t            name: tool.name,\n193\t            description: tool.description,\n194\t            input_schema: tool.input_schema,\n195\t        }\n196\t    }\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t/// Trait for AI chat providers.\n200\t///\n201\t/// Implementations can use different backends:\n202\t/// - HTTP APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.)\n203\t/// - CLI tools (claude-cli, aider, etc.)\n204\t/// - Mock providers for testing\n205\t#[async_trait]\n206\tpub trait ChatProvider: Send + Sync {\n207\t    /// Process a prompt with context and tools.\n208\t    ///\n209\t    /// The provider may invoke tools during processing. Tool results are\n210\t    /// NOT included in the response - callers must handle tool execution\n211\t    /// separately based on the returned tool_calls.\n212\t    ///\n213\t    /// # Arguments\n214\t    /// * `prompt` - The user's prompt/command\n215\t    /// * `context` - Current dashboard context for contextual awareness\n216\t    /// * `tools` - Available tools that the provider may invoke\n217\t    async fn process(\n218\t        &self,\n219\t        prompt: &str,\n220\t        context: &DashboardContext,\n221\t        tools: &[ProviderTool],\n222\t    ) -> Result<ProviderResponse>;\n223\t\n224\t    /// Get the provider name for logging/debugging.\n225\t    fn name(&self) -> &str;\n226\t\n227\t    /// Whether this provider supports streaming responses.\n228\t    fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n229\t        false\n230\t    }\n231\t\n232\t    /// Estimated cost per request (if known).\n233\t    ///\n234\t    /// Returns None if cost estimation is not available for this provider.\n235\t    /// This is useful for cost tracking and budget management.\n236\t    fn estimated_cost(&self) -> Option<f64> {\n237\t        None\n238\t    }\n239\t\n240\t    /// Get the model name being used.\n241\t    fn model(&self) -> &str;\n242\t}\n243\t\n244\t// ============ Claude CLI Provider ============\n245\t// Note: Claude API provider has been moved to claude_api.rs module\n246\t\n247\t/// Claude CLI provider using stdin/stdout communication.\n248\t///\n249\t/// This provider spawns a claude-cli process and communicates via JSON messages.\n250\tpub struct ClaudeCliProvider {\n251\t    config: ClaudeCliConfig,\n252\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n253\t    process: Mutex<Option<CliProcess>>,\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\t/// Represents a running claude-cli process.\n257\tstruct CliProcess {\n258\t    child: tokio::process::Child,\n259\t    stdin: tokio::process::ChildStdin,\n260\t    stdout: tokio::process::ChildStdout,\n261\t    stderr: tokio::process::ChildStderr,\n262\t}\n263\t\n264\timpl ClaudeCliProvider {\n265\t    /// Create a new Claude CLI provider.\n266\t    pub fn new(config: ClaudeCliConfig) -> Self {\n267\t        Self {\n268\t            config,\n269\t            process: Mutex::new(None),\n270\t        }\n271\t    }\n272\t\n273\t    /// Create a provider from a ChatConfig (deprecated - use ProviderConfig directly).\n274\t    #[deprecated(note = \"Use ProviderConfig::ClaudeCli directly instead\")]\n275\t    pub fn from_chat_config(_config: &ChatConfig) -> Self {\n276\t        Self::new(ClaudeCliConfig::default())\n277\t    }\n278\t\n279\t    /// Spawn the claude-cli process.\n280\t    async fn spawn_process(&self) -> Result<CliProcess> {\n281\t        let mut cmd = Command::new(&self.config.binary_path);\n282\t\n283\t        // Add model flag\n284\t        cmd.arg(\"--model\").arg(&self.config.model);\n285\t\n286\t        // Add headless flag if configured\n287\t        if self.config.headless {\n288\t            cmd.arg(\"--headless\");\n289\t        }\n290\t\n291\t        // Add extra args\n292\t        cmd.args(&self.config.extra_args);\n293\t\n294\t        // Set up stdin/stdout/stderr for JSON communication\n295\t        cmd.stdin(Stdio::piped())\n296\t            .stdout(Stdio::piped())\n297\t            .stderr(Stdio::piped());\n298\t\n299\t        info!(\"Spawning claude-cli: {:?}\", cmd);\n300\t\n301\t        let mut child = cmd\n302\t            .spawn()\n303\t            .map_err(|e| ChatError::ConfigError(format!(\"Failed to spawn claude-cli: {}\", e)))?;\n304\t\n305\t        let stdin = child.stdin.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n306\t            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stdin for claude-cli\".to_string())\n307\t        })?;\n308\t\n309\t        let stdout = child.stdout.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n310\t            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stdout for claude-cli\".to_string())\n311\t        })?;\n312\t\n313\t        let stderr = child.stderr.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n314\t            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stderr for claude-cli\".to_string())\n315\t        })?;\n316\t\n317\t        Ok(CliProcess {\n318\t            child,\n319\t            stdin,\n320\t            stdout,\n321\t            stderr,\n322\t        })\n323\t    }\n324\t\n325\t    /// Send a request to claude-cli and read the response.\n326\t    async fn send_request(&self, prompt: &str, tools: &[ProviderTool]) -> Result<CliResponse> {\n327\t        // Spawn a new process for each request (simpler reliability)\n328\t        let proc = self.spawn_process().await?;\n329\t\n330\t        let CliProcess {\n331\t            mut child,\n332\t            mut stdin,\n333\t            stdout,\n334\t            mut stderr,\n335\t        } = proc;\n336\t\n337\t        // Build the request JSON\n338\t        let request = CliRequest {\n339\t            prompt: prompt.to_string(),\n340\t            tools: tools.to_vec(),\n341\t        };\n342\t\n343\t        let request_json = serde_json::to_string(&request).map_err(ChatError::JsonError)?;\n344\t\n345\t        // Send request\n346\t        use tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt;\n347\t        stdin\n348\t            .write_all(request_json.as_bytes())\n349\t            .await\n350\t            .map_err(ChatError::IoError)?;\n351\t        stdin.write_all(b\"\\n\").await.map_err(ChatError::IoError)?;\n352\t        stdin.shutdown().await.map_err(ChatError::IoError)?;\n353\t\n354\t        // Read response with timeout\n355\t        let response_bytes = timeout(Duration::from_secs(30), async {\n356\t            use tokio::io::AsyncBufReadExt;\n357\t            let mut reader = tokio::io::BufReader::new(stdout);\n358\t            let mut line = String::new();\n359\t            reader.read_line(&mut line).await?;\n360\t            Ok::<Vec<u8>, ChatError>(line.into_bytes())\n361\t        })\n362\t        .await\n363\t        .map_err(|_| {\n364\t            // Kill the child process on timeout\n365\t            drop(child.kill());\n366\t            ChatError::ApiError(\"claude-cli timeout\".to_string())\n367\t        })??;\n368\t\n369\t        // Also capture stderr for debugging\n370\t        let stderr_output = timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), async {\n371\t            use tokio::io::AsyncReadExt;\n372\t            let mut buf = vec![];\n373\t            stderr.read_to_end(&mut buf).await?;\n374\t            Ok::<Vec<u8>, ChatError>(buf)\n375\t        })\n376\t        .await\n377\t        .unwrap_or(Ok(vec![]))?;\n378\t\n379\t        if !stderr_output.is_empty() {\n380\t            let stderr_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&stderr_output);\n381\t            debug!(\"claude-cli stderr: {}\", stderr_str);\n382\t        }\n383\t\n384\t        // Wait for process to exit\n385\t        let status = timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), child.wait())\n386\t            .await\n387\t            .map_err(|_| ChatError::ApiError(\"claude-cli hang on exit\".to_string()))??;\n388\t\n389\t        if !status.success() {\n390\t            return Err(ChatError::ApiError(format!(\n391\t                \"claude-cli exited with status: {:?}\",\n392\t                status\n393\t            )));\n394\t        }\n395\t\n396\t        // Parse response\n397\t        let response_str = String::from_utf8(response_bytes)\n398\t            .map_err(|e| ChatError::ApiError(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8 from claude-cli: {}\", e)))?;\n399\t\n400\t        debug!(\"claude-cli response: {}\", response_str);\n401\t\n402\t        let response: CliResponse = serde_json::from_str(&response_str).map_err(|e| {\n403\t            ChatError::ApiError(format!(\"Failed to parse claude-cli response: {}\", e))\n404\t        })?;\n405\t\n406\t        Ok(response)\n407\t    }\n408\t}\n409\t\n410\t#[async_trait]\n411\timpl ChatProvider for ClaudeCliProvider {\n412\t    async fn process(\n413\t        &self,\n414\t        prompt: &str,\n415\t        context: &DashboardContext,\n416\t        tools: &[ProviderTool],\n417\t    ) -> Result<ProviderResponse> {\n418\t        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n419\t\n420\t        // Build the enhanced prompt with context\n421\t        let enhanced_prompt = format!(\n422\t            \"{}\\n\\nCurrent dashboard state:\\n{}\",\n423\t            prompt,\n424\t            context.to_summary()\n425\t        );\n426\t\n427\t        let cli_response = self.send_request(&enhanced_prompt, tools).await?;\n428\t\n429\t        let duration = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n430\t\n431\t        // Convert tool calls\n432\t        let tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall> = cli_response\n433\t            .tool_calls\n434\t            .into_iter()\n435\t            .map(|tc| ToolCall {\n436\t                name: tc.name,\n437\t                parameters: tc.parameters,\n438\t                id: tc.id,\n439\t            })\n440\t            .collect();\n441\t\n442\t        let finish_reason = if !tool_calls.is_empty() {\n443\t            FinishReason::ToolCall\n444\t        } else {\n445\t            FinishReason::Stop\n446\t        };\n447\t\n448\t        Ok(ProviderResponse {\n449\t            text: cli_response.text,\n450\t            tool_calls,\n451\t            duration_ms: duration,\n452\t            cost_usd: Some(0.0), // CLI provider doesn't report cost\n453\t            finish_reason,\n454\t            usage: None, // CLI doesn't provide token usage\n455\t        })\n456\t    }\n457\t\n458\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n459\t        \"claude-cli\"\n460\t    }\n461\t\n462\t    fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n463\t        false\n464\t    }\n465\t\n466\t    fn model(&self) -> &str {\n467\t        &self.config.model\n468\t    }\n469\t}\n470\t\n471\t/// Request sent to claude-cli.\n472\t#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\n473\tstruct CliRequest {\n474\t    prompt: String,\n475\t    tools: Vec<ProviderTool>,\n476\t}\n477\t\n478\t/// Response received from claude-cli.\n479\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n480\tstruct CliResponse {\n481\t    text: String,\n482\t    #[serde(default)]\n483\t    tool_calls: Vec<CliToolCall>,\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t/// Tool call from claude-cli.\n487\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n488\tstruct CliToolCall {\n489\t    name: String,\n490\t    parameters: serde_json::Value,\n491\t    #[serde(default)]\n492\t    id: Option<String>,\n493\t}\n494\t\n495\t// ============ Mock Provider ============\n496\t\n497\t/// Mock provider for testing.\n498\t///\n499\t/// Returns predefined responses without making any external calls.\n500\t///\n501\t/// # Features\n502\t///\n503\t/// - Call tracking: records all prompts sent to the provider\n504\t/// - Multiple responses: can return different responses for sequential calls\n505\t/// - Tool call simulation: can simulate tool calls in responses\n506\t/// - Error simulation: can simulate errors for testing error handling\n507\t///\n508\t/// # Example\n509\t///\n510\t/// ```no_run\n511\t/// use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n512\t///\n513\t/// let provider = MockProvider::new()\n514\t///     .with_response(\"First response\")\n515\t///     .and_then_response(\"Second response\")\n516\t///     .and_then_error(\"API error\");\n517\t///\n518\t/// // First call returns \"First response\"\n519\t/// // Second call returns \"Second response\"\n520\t/// // Third call returns an error\n521\t/// ```\n522\tpub struct MockProvider {\n523\t    model: String,\n524\t    responses: Arc<Mutex<Vec<MockResponse>>>,\n525\t    calls: Arc<Mutex<Vec<MockCall>>>,\n526\t    response_delay_ms: u64,\n527\t    simulate_error_after: Option<(usize, String)>,\n528\t}\n529\t\n530\t/// A mock response that can be returned by the MockProvider.\n531\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n532\tstruct MockResponse {\n533\t    text: String,\n534\t    tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>,\n535\t    is_error: bool,\n536\t    error_message: Option<String>,\n537\t}\n538\t\n539\timpl MockResponse {\n540\t    fn success(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n541\t        Self {\n542\t            text: text.into(),\n543\t            tool_calls: vec![],\n544\t            is_error: false,\n545\t            error_message: None,\n546\t        }\n547\t    }\n548\t\n549\t    fn with_tool_call(mut self, name: impl Into<String>, parameters: serde_json::Value) -> Self {\n550\t        self.tool_calls.push(ToolCall {\n551\t            name: name.into(),\n552\t            parameters,\n553\t            id: Some(format!(\"mock-tool-{}\", self.tool_calls.len())),\n554\t        });\n555\t        self\n556\t    }\n557\t\n558\t    fn error(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n559\t        Self {\n560\t            text: String::new(),\n561\t            tool_calls: vec![],\n562\t            is_error: true,\n563\t            error_message: Some(message.into()),\n564\t        }\n565\t    }\n566\t}\n567\t\n568\t/// Record of a call made to the MockProvider.\n569\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n570\tpub struct MockCall {\n571\t    /// The prompt that was sent.\n572\t    pub prompt: String,\n573\t    /// The tools that were available.\n574\t    pub tools_available: usize,\n575\t    /// Timestamp of the call.\n576\t    #[serde(skip)]\n577\t    pub timestamp: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\timpl MockProvider {\n581\t    /// Create a new mock provider with default responses.\n582\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n583\t        Self::from_config(MockConfig::default())\n584\t    }\n585\t\n586\t    /// Create a mock provider from config.\n587\t    pub fn from_config(config: MockConfig) -> Self {\n588\t        Self {\n589\t            model: config.model,\n590\t            responses: Arc::new(Mutex::new(vec![MockResponse::success(config.response)])),\n591\t            calls: Arc::new(Mutex::new(vec![])),\n592\t            response_delay_ms: config.delay_ms,\n593\t            simulate_error_after: None,\n594\t        }\n595\t    }\n596\t\n597\t    /// Set a single response text (replaces all queued responses).\n598\t    pub fn with_response(mut self, text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n599\t        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(vec![MockResponse::success(text.into())]));\n600\t        self\n601\t    }\n602\t\n603\t    /// Set multiple responses at once.\n604\t    ///\n605\t    /// # Example\n606\t    ///\n607\t    /// ```no_run\n608\t    /// # use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n609\t    /// let provider = MockProvider::new()\n610\t    ///     .with_multiple_responses([\"First\", \"Second\", \"Third\"]);\n611\t    /// ```\n612\t    pub fn with_multiple_responses<I>(mut self, responses: I) -> Self\n613\t    where\n614\t        I: IntoIterator,\n615\t        I::Item: Into<String>,\n616\t    {\n617\t        let resp: Vec<MockResponse> = responses\n618\t            .into_iter()\n619\t            .map(|s| MockResponse::success(s.into()))\n620\t            .collect();\n621\t        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(resp));\n622\t        self\n623\t    }\n624\t\n625\t    /// Add another response to the queue (chains with current responses).\n626\t    ///\n627\t    /// Note: This method may not work correctly when the provider is already\n628\t    /// shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. For reliable multi-response setup, use\n629\t    /// `with_multiple_responses` for initial configuration, or `add_response`\n630\t    /// for dynamic modification after sharing.\n631\t    ///\n632\t    /// For modifying responses on a shared provider, use `add_response` instead.\n633\t    pub fn and_then_response(mut self, text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n634\t        // Clone the Arc first to avoid borrow issues\n635\t        let responses_arc = self.responses.clone();\n636\t        let mut current = match responses_arc.try_lock() {\n637\t            Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n638\t            Err(_) => {\n639\t                // Lock failed, can't chain\n640\t                return self;\n641\t            }\n642\t        };\n643\t\n644\t        current.push(MockResponse::success(text.into()));\n645\t        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(current));\n646\t        self\n647\t    }\n648\t\n649\t    /// Add a tool call response to the queue.\n650\t    ///\n651\t    /// Note: This method may not work correctly when the provider is already\n652\t    /// shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. For dynamic tool call chaining on shared\n653\t    /// providers, use `add_tool_call_response` instead.\n654\t    ///\n655\t    /// For modifying responses on a shared provider, use `add_tool_call_response`.\n656\t    pub fn and_then_tool_call(\n657\t        mut self,\n658\t        tool_name: impl Into<String>,\n659\t        parameters: serde_json::Value,\n660\t    ) -> Self {\n661\t        let responses_arc = self.responses.clone();\n662\t        let mut current = match responses_arc.try_lock() {\n663\t            Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n664\t            Err(_) => return self,\n665\t        };\n666\t\n667\t        current.push(MockResponse::success(\"\").with_tool_call(tool_name, parameters));\n668\t        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(current));\n669\t        self\n670\t    }\n671\t\n672\t    /// Add an error response to the queue.\n673\t    ///\n674\t    /// Note: This method may not work correctly when the provider is already\n675\t    /// shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. For dynamic error chaining on shared providers,\n676\t    /// use `add_error` instead.\n677\t    ///\n678\t    /// For modifying responses on a shared provider, use `add_error`.\n679\t    pub fn and_then_error(mut self, error_message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n680\t        let responses_arc = self.responses.clone();\n681\t        let mut current = match responses_arc.try_lock() {\n682\t            Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n683\t            Err(_) => return self,\n684\t        };\n685\t\n686\t        current.push(MockResponse::error(error_message));\n687\t        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(current));\n688\t        self\n689\t    }\n690\t\n691\t    // ============ Methods for Shared Provider (Arc<Mutex<>>) Pattern ============\n692\t    //\n693\t    // The following methods work on &self and can be used when the provider\n694\t    // is shared via Arc<Mutex<MockProvider>>. Use these for dynamic response\n695\t    // chaining after the provider has been shared.\n696\t\n697\t    /// Add a tool call response to the queue (works on shared references).\n698\t    ///\n699\t    /// Unlike `and_then_tool_call`, this method works on `&self` and can be called\n700\t    /// even when the provider is shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. It modifies the shared\n701\t    /// response queue directly through the mutex.\n702\t    ///\n703\t    /// # Example\n704\t    ///\n705\t    /// ```no_run\n706\t    /// # use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n707\t    /// # use std::sync::Arc;\n708\t    /// # async fn example() {\n709\t    /// let provider = Arc::new(MockProvider::new());\n710\t    /// let provider_ref = provider.clone();\n711\t    ///\n712\t    /// // This works because add_tool_call_response takes &self\n713\t    /// provider_ref.add_tool_call_response(\"tool_name\".to_string(), serde_json::json!({})).await;\n714\t    /// # }\n715\t    /// ```\n716\t    pub async fn add_tool_call_response(&self, tool_name: String, parameters: serde_json::Value) {\n717\t        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n718\t        responses.push(MockResponse::success(\"\").with_tool_call(tool_name, parameters));\n719\t    }\n720\t\n721\t    /// Add a text response to the queue (works on shared references).\n722\t    ///\n723\t    /// Unlike `and_then_response`, this method works on `&self` and can be called\n724\t    /// even when the provider is shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`.\n725\t    pub async fn add_response(&self, text: String) {\n726\t        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n727\t        responses.push(MockResponse::success(text));\n728\t    }\n729\t\n730\t    /// Add an error response to the queue (works on shared references).\n731\t    ///\n732\t    /// Unlike `and_then_error`, this method works on `&self` and can be called\n733\t    /// even when the provider is shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`.\n734\t    pub async fn add_error(&self, error_message: String) {\n735\t        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n736\t        responses.push(MockResponse::error(error_message));\n737\t    }\n738\t\n739\t    /// Add multiple responses to the queue (works on shared references).\n740\t    ///\n741\t    /// # Example\n742\t    ///\n743\t    /// ```no_run\n744\t    /// # use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n745\t    /// # async fn example() {\n746\t    /// let provider = MockProvider::new();\n747\t    /// provider.add_multiple_responses([\"First\", \"Second\", \"Third\"]).await;\n748\t    /// # }\n749\t    /// ```\n750\t    pub async fn add_multiple_responses<I>(&self, responses: I)\n751\t    where\n752\t        I: IntoIterator,\n753\t        I::Item: Into<String>,\n754\t    {\n755\t        let mut mock_responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n756\t        for text in responses {\n757\t            mock_responses.push(MockResponse::success(text.into()));\n758\t        }\n759\t    }\n760\t\n761\t    /// Clear all responses from the queue.\n762\t    ///\n763\t    /// This is useful when you want to start fresh without the default response.\n764\t    ///\n765\t    /// # Example\n766\t    ///\n767\t    /// ```no_run\n768\t    /// # use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n769\t    /// # async fn example() {\n770\t    /// let provider = MockProvider::new();\n771\t    /// provider.clear_responses().await;\n772\t    /// provider.add_response(\"Custom response\".to_string()).await;\n773\t    /// # }\n774\t    /// ```\n775\t    pub async fn clear_responses(&self) {\n776\t        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n777\t        responses.clear();\n778\t    }\n779\t\n780\t    /// Simulate errors after N calls.\n781\t    pub fn with_error_after(mut self, count: usize, error_message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n782\t        self.simulate_error_after = Some((count, error_message.into()));\n783\t        self\n784\t    }\n785\t\n786\t    /// Set the response delay.\n787\t    pub fn with_delay(mut self, delay_ms: u64) -> Self {\n788\t        self.response_delay_ms = delay_ms;\n789\t        self\n790\t    }\n791\t\n792\t    /// Set the model name.\n793\t    pub fn with_model(mut self, model: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n794\t        self.model = model.into();\n795\t        self\n796\t    }\n797\t\n798\t    /// Get the recorded calls.\n799\t    pub async fn get_calls(&self) -> Vec<MockCall> {\n800\t        self.calls.lock().await.clone()\n801\t    }\n802\t\n803\t    /// Get the number of calls made.\n804\t    pub async fn call_count(&self) -> usize {\n805\t        self.calls.lock().await.len()\n806\t    }\n807\t\n808\t    /// Clear the call history.\n809\t    pub async fn clear_calls(&self) {\n810\t        self.calls.lock().await.clear();\n811\t    }\n812\t\n813\t    /// Check if a specific prompt was called.\n814\t    pub async fn was_called_with(&self, prompt: &str) -> bool {\n815\t        let calls = self.calls.lock().await;\n816\t        calls.iter().any(|c| c.prompt.contains(prompt))\n817\t    }\n818\t}\n819\t\n820\timpl Default for MockProvider {\n821\t    fn default() -> Self {\n822\t        Self::new()\n823\t    }\n824\t}\n825\t\n826\t#[async_trait]\n827\timpl ChatProvider for MockProvider {\n828\t    async fn process(\n829\t        &self,\n830\t        prompt: &str,\n831\t        _context: &DashboardContext,\n832\t        tools: &[ProviderTool],\n833\t    ) -> Result<ProviderResponse> {\n834\t        // Record the call\n835\t        let call = MockCall {\n836\t            prompt: prompt.to_string(),\n837\t            tools_available: tools.len(),\n838\t            timestamp: Utc::now(),\n839\t        };\n840\t        self.calls.lock().await.push(call);\n841\t\n842\t        // Check if we should simulate an error\n843\t        if let Some((count, ref error_msg)) = self.simulate_error_after {\n844\t            let current_count = self.calls.lock().await.len();\n845\t            if current_count > count {\n846\t                return Err(ChatError::ApiError(error_msg.clone()));\n847\t            }\n848\t        }\n849\t\n850\t        // Simulate delay\n851\t        if self.response_delay_ms > 0 {\n852\t            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(self.response_delay_ms)).await;\n853\t        }\n854\t\n855\t        // Get the next response\n856\t        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n857\t        let response = if responses.is_empty() {\n858\t            // If no responses queued, return a default\n859\t            MockResponse::success(\"Mock response\")\n860\t        } else if responses.len() == 1 {\n861\t            // If only one response, reuse it (for single-response providers)\n862\t            responses[0].clone()\n863\t        } else {\n864\t            // If multiple responses, consume one and return it\n865\t            responses.remove(0)\n866\t        };\n867\t\n868\t        // Check if this is an error response\n869\t        if response.is_error {\n870\t            return Err(ChatError::ApiError(\n871\t                response\n872\t                    .error_message\n873\t                    .unwrap_or_else(|| \"Mock error\".to_string()),\n874\t            ));\n875\t        }\n876\t\n877\t        Ok(ProviderResponse {\n878\t            text: response.text,\n879\t            tool_calls: response.tool_calls,\n880\t            duration_ms: self.response_delay_ms,\n881\t            cost_usd: Some(0.0),\n882\t            finish_reason: FinishReason::Stop,\n883\t            usage: None,\n884\t        })\n885\t    }\n886\t\n887\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n888\t        \"mock\"\n889\t    }\n890\t\n891\t    fn estimated_cost(&self) -> Option<f64> {\n892\t        Some(0.0)\n893\t    }\n894\t\n895\t    fn model(&self) -> &str {\n896\t        &self.model\n897\t    }\n898\t}\n899\t\n900\t// ============ Opencode CLI Provider ============\n901\t\n902\t/// Opencode CLI provider using subprocess communication.\n903\t///\n904\t/// This provider spawns an opencode process with `--format json` and parses\n905\t/// the streaming JSON output events.\n906\t///\n907\t/// # Output Format\n908\t///\n909\t/// Opencode outputs JSON lines with the following event types:\n910\t/// - `step_start`: Indicates a new step is starting\n911\t/// - `text`: Contains actual text output in `part.text`\n912\t/// - `step_finish`: Indicates step completion with token usage\n913\tpub struct OpencodeProvider {\n914\t    config: OpencodeConfig,\n915\t}\n916\t\n917\timpl OpencodeProvider {\n918\t    /// Create a new Opencode provider.\n919\t    pub fn new(config: OpencodeConfig) -> Self {\n920\t        Self { config }\n921\t    }\n922\t\n923\t    /// Create a provider from a ChatConfig.\n924\t    pub fn from_chat_config(_config: &ChatConfig) -> Self {\n925\t        Self::new(OpencodeConfig::default())\n926\t    }\n927\t\n928\t    /// Send a request to opencode and parse the streaming JSON response.\n929\t    async fn send_request(&self, prompt: &str) -> Result<OpencodeResponse> {\n930\t        let mut cmd = Command::new(&self.config.binary_path);\n931\t\n932\t        // Build command: opencode run --format json --model <model> \"<prompt>\"\n933\t        cmd.args([\"run\", \"--format\", \"json\", \"--model\", &self.config.model])\n934\t            .arg(prompt);\n935\t\n936\t        // Set up pipes\n937\t        cmd.stdin(Stdio::null())\n938\t            .stdout(Stdio::piped())\n939\t            .stderr(Stdio::piped());\n940\t\n941\t        info!(\"Spawning opencode: {:?}\", cmd);\n942\t\n943\t        let mut child = cmd\n944\t            .spawn()\n945\t            .map_err(|e| ChatError::ConfigError(format!(\"Failed to spawn opencode: {}\", e)))?;\n946\t\n947\t        let stdout = child.stdout.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n948\t            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stdout for opencode\".to_string())\n949\t        })?;\n950\t\n951\t        let stderr = child.stderr.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n952\t            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stderr for opencode\".to_string())\n953\t        })?;\n954\t\n955\t        // Read stdout with timeout\n956\t        let stdout_bytes = timeout(Duration::from_secs(self.config.timeout_secs), async {\n957\t            use tokio::io::AsyncReadExt;\n958\t            let mut buf = vec![];\n959\t            let mut stdout = stdout;\n960\t            stdout.read_to_end(&mut buf).await?;\n961\t            Ok::<Vec<u8>, std::io::Error>(buf)\n962\t        })\n963\t        .await\n964\t        .map_err(|_| {\n965\t            drop(child.kill());\n966\t            ChatError::ApiError(\"opencode timeout\".to_string())\n967\t        })?\n968\t        .map_err(ChatError::IoError)?;\n969\t\n970\t        // Read stderr for debugging\n971\t        let stderr_bytes = timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), async {\n972\t            use tokio::io::AsyncReadExt;\n973\t            let mut buf = vec![];\n974\t            let mut stderr = stderr;\n975\t            stderr.read_to_end(&mut buf).await?;\n976\t            Ok::<Vec<u8>, std::io::Error>(buf)\n977\t        })\n978\t        .await\n979\t        .unwrap_or(Ok(vec![]))\n980\t        .unwrap_or_default();\n981\t\n982\t        // Wait for process to exit\n983\t        let status = timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), child.wait())\n984\t            .await\n985\t            .map_err(|_| ChatError::ApiError(\"opencode hang on exit\".to_string()))??;\n986\t\n987\t        if !status.success() {\n988\t            let stderr_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&stderr_bytes);\n989\t            return Err(ChatError::ApiError(format!(\n990\t                \"opencode exited with status: {:?}, stderr: {}\",\n991\t                status, stderr_str\n992\t            )));\n993\t        }\n994\t\n995\t        // Parse the streaming JSON response\n996\t        let stdout_str = String::from_utf8(stdout_bytes)\n997\t            .map_err(|e| ChatError::ApiError(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8 from opencode: {}\", e)))?;\n998\t\n999\t        debug!(\"opencode raw output: {}\", stdout_str);\n1000\t\n1001\t        self.parse_opencode_response(&stdout_str)\n1002\t    }\n1003\t\n1004\t    /// Parse opencode streaming JSON response.\n1005\t    ///\n1006\t    /// The response consists of JSON lines with events:\n1007\t    /// - {\"type\":\"step_start\",...}\n1008\t    /// - {\"type\":\"text\",\"part\":{\"text\":\"Hello there\"},...}\n1009\t    /// - {\"type\":\"step_finish\",\"part\":{\"tokens\":{...},\"cost\":0},...}\n1010\t    fn parse_opencode_response(&self, output: &str) -> Result<OpencodeResponse> {\n1011\t        let mut text_parts = Vec::new();\n1012\t        let mut total_cost = 0.0;\n1013\t        let mut usage = TokenUsage::zero();\n1014\t\n1015\t        for line in output.lines() {\n1016\t            let line = line.trim();\n1017\t            if line.is_empty() {\n1018\t                continue;\n1019\t            }\n1020\t\n1021\t            // Parse each JSON line\n1022\t            let event: OpencodeEvent = match serde_json::from_str(line) {\n1023\t                Ok(e) => e,\n1024\t                Err(e) => {\n1025\t                    debug!(\"Failed to parse opencode line as event: {} - {}\", line, e);\n1026\t                    continue;\n1027\t                }\n1028\t            };\n1029\t\n1030\t            match event.event_type.as_str() {\n1031\t                \"text\" => {\n1032\t                    // Extract text from the part.text field\n1033\t                    if let Some(part) = event.part\n1034\t                        && let Some(text) = part.text\n1035\t                        && part.synthetic != Some(true)\n1036\t                    {\n1037\t                        // Skip synthetic messages (like \"Continue if you have next steps\")\n1038\t                        text_parts.push(text);\n1039\t                    }\n1040\t                }\n1041\t                \"step_finish\" => {\n1042\t                    // Extract cost and token usage\n1043\t                    if let Some(part) = event.part {\n1044\t                        if let Some(cost) = part.cost {\n1045\t                            total_cost += cost;\n1046\t                        }\n1047\t                        if let Some(tokens) = part.tokens {\n1048\t                            usage.input_tokens = tokens.input.unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n1049\t                            usage.output_tokens = tokens.output.unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n1050\t                        }\n1051\t                    }\n1052\t                }\n1053\t                _ => {\n1054\t                    // Ignore step_start and other events\n1055\t                }\n1056\t            }\n1057\t        }\n1058\t\n1059\t        // Combine all text parts\n1060\t        let full_text = text_parts.join(\"\\n\");\n1061\t\n1062\t        Ok(OpencodeResponse {\n1063\t            text: full_text,\n1064\t            cost: total_cost,\n1065\t            usage,\n1066\t        })\n1067\t    }\n1068\t}\n1069\t\n1070\t/// Response from opencode CLI.\n1071\tstruct OpencodeResponse {\n1072\t    text: String,\n1073\t    cost: f64,\n1074\t    usage: TokenUsage,\n1075\t}\n1076\t\n1077\t/// Opencode streaming event types.\n1078\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n1079\tstruct OpencodeEvent {\n1080\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n1081\t    event_type: String,\n1082\t    part: Option<OpencodePart>,\n1083\t}\n1084\t\n1085\t/// Opencode event part data.\n1086\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n1087\tstruct OpencodePart {\n1088\t    text: Option<String>,\n1089\t    #[serde(default)]\n1090\t    synthetic: Option<bool>,\n1091\t    cost: Option<f64>,\n1092\t    tokens: Option<OpencodeTokens>,\n1093\t}\n1094\t\n1095\t/// Token usage from opencode.\n1096\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n1097\tstruct OpencodeTokens {\n1098\t    input: Option<u64>,\n1099\t    output: Option<u64>,\n1100\t}\n1101\t\n1102\t#[async_trait]\n1103\timpl ChatProvider for OpencodeProvider {\n1104\t    async fn process(\n1105\t        &self,\n1106\t        prompt: &str,\n1107\t        context: &DashboardContext,\n1108\t        _tools: &[ProviderTool],\n1109\t    ) -> Result<ProviderResponse> {\n1110\t        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n1111\t\n1112\t        // Build the enhanced prompt with context\n1113\t        let enhanced_prompt = format!(\n1114\t            \"{}\\n\\nCurrent dashboard state:\\n{}\",\n1115\t            prompt,\n1116\t            context.to_summary()\n1117\t        );\n1118\t\n1119\t        let response = self.send_request(&enhanced_prompt).await?;\n1120\t\n1121\t        let duration = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n1122\t\n1123\t        Ok(ProviderResponse {\n1124\t            text: response.text,\n1125\t            tool_calls: vec![],\n1126\t            duration_ms: duration,\n1127\t            cost_usd: Some(response.cost),\n1128\t            finish_reason: FinishReason::Stop,\n1129\t            usage: Some(response.usage),\n1130\t        })\n1131\t    }\n1132\t\n1133\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n1134\t        \"opencode\"\n1135\t    }\n1136\t\n1137\t    fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n1138\t        false\n1139\t    }\n1140\t\n1141\t    fn model(&self) -> &str {\n1142\t        &self.config.model\n1143\t    }\n1144\t}\n1145\t\n1146\t// ============ Provider Factory ============\n1147\t\n1148\t/// Create a provider from configuration.\n1149\t///\n1150\t/// This factory function creates the appropriate provider based on the\n1151\t/// `ProviderConfig` in `ChatConfig`.\n1152\t///\n1153\t/// # Example\n1154\t///\n1155\t/// ```no_run\n1156\t/// use forge_chat::{ChatConfig, provider::create_provider};\n1157\t///\n1158\t/// # fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n1159\t/// let config = ChatConfig::default();\n1160\t/// let provider = create_provider(&config)?;\n1161\t/// # Ok(())\n1162\t/// # }\n1163\t/// ```\n1164\tpub fn create_provider(config: &ChatConfig) -> Result<Box<dyn ChatProvider>> {\n1165\t    // Check for environment variable override (for backward compatibility)\n1166\t    if let Ok(provider_type) = std::env::var(\"FORGE_CHAT_PROVIDER\") {\n1167\t        info!(\n1168\t            \"Using FORGE_CHAT_PROVIDER environment variable: {}\",\n1169\t            provider_type\n1170\t        );\n1171\t        return create_provider_by_type(&provider_type, config);\n1172\t    }\n1173\t\n1174\t    // Use the provider from config\n1175\t    match &config.provider {\n1176\t        ProviderConfig::ClaudeApi(api_config) => {\n1177\t            info!(\"Creating claude-api provider\");\n1178\t            Ok(Box::new(ClaudeApiProvider::from_config(\n1179\t                api_config.clone(),\n1180\t            )?))\n1181\t        }\n1182\t        ProviderConfig::ClaudeCli(cli_config) => {\n1183\t            info!(\"Creating claude-cli provider\");\n1184\t            Ok(Box::new(ClaudeCliProvider::new(cli_config.clone())))\n1185\t        }\n1186\t        ProviderConfig::Opencode(opencode_config) => {\n1187\t            info!(\"Creating opencode provider\");\n1188\t            Ok(Box::new(OpencodeProvider::new(opencode_config.clone())))\n1189\t        }\n1190\t        ProviderConfig::Mock(mock_config) => {\n1191\t            info!(\"Creating mock provider\");\n1192\t            Ok(Box::new(MockProvider::from_config(mock_config.clone())))\n1193\t        }\n1194\t    }\n1195\t}\n1196\t\n1197\t/// Create a provider by type string (for backward compatibility with env var).\n1198\tfn create_provider_by_type(\n1199\t    provider_type: &str,\n1200\t    _config: &ChatConfig,\n1201\t) -> Result<Box<dyn ChatProvider>> {\n1202\t    match provider_type {\n1203\t        \"claude-cli\" => {\n1204\t            info!(\"Creating claude-cli provider from env var\");\n1205\t            Ok(Box::new(ClaudeCliProvider::new(ClaudeCliConfig::default())))\n1206\t        }\n1207\t        \"opencode\" => {\n1208\t            info!(\"Creating opencode provider from env var\");\n1209\t            Ok(Box::new(OpencodeProvider::new(OpencodeConfig::default())))\n1210\t        }\n1211\t        \"mock\" => {\n1212\t            info!(\"Creating mock provider from env var\");\n1213\t            Ok(Box::new(MockProvider::new()))\n1214\t        }\n1215\t        _ => {\n1216\t            info!(\"Creating claude-api provider from env var\");\n1217\t            Ok(Box::new(ClaudeApiProvider::from_config(\n1218\t                ClaudeApiConfig::default(),\n1219\t            )?))\n1220\t        }\n1221\t    }\n1222\t}\n1223\t\n1224\t/// Factory for creating chat providers based on a provider name string.\n1225\t///\n1226\t/// Reads the `provider` field from configuration and instantiates the appropriate\n1227\t/// [`ProviderConfig`]. Defaults to `\"claude-cli\"` for backwards compatibility.\n1228\t///\n1229\t/// # Example\n1230\t///\n1231\t/// ```no_run\n1232\t/// use forge_chat::provider::ChatProviderFactory;\n1233\t///\n1234\t/// # fn main() -> forge_chat::error::Result<()> {\n1235\t/// let config = ChatProviderFactory::create_config(\"opencode\", \"opencode\", \"sonnet\", vec![], None)?;\n1236\t/// # Ok(())\n1237\t/// # }\n1238\t/// ```\n1239\tpub struct ChatProviderFactory;\n1240\t\n1241\timpl ChatProviderFactory {\n1242\t    /// Create a [`ProviderConfig`] from a provider name string.\n1243\t    ///\n1244\t    /// # Arguments\n1245\t    ///\n1246\t    /// - `provider_name` — `\"opencode\"` or `\"claude-cli\"`. An empty string falls\n1247\t    ///   back to `\"claude-cli\"` for backwards compatibility.\n1248\t    /// - `binary_path` — Path/name of the provider binary.\n1249\t    /// - `model` — Model identifier to use.\n1250\t    /// - `extra_args` — Additional CLI arguments (used by `claude-cli`).\n1251\t    /// - `model_aliases` — Model alias map (used by `opencode`).\n1252\t    ///\n1253\t    /// # Errors\n1254\t    ///\n1255\t    /// Returns [`ChatError::ConfigError`] if `provider_name` is not recognised.\n1256\t    pub fn create_config(\n1257\t        provider_name: &str,\n1258\t        binary_path: &str,\n1259\t        model: &str,\n1260\t        extra_args: Vec<String>,\n1261\t        model_aliases: Option<std::collections::HashMap<String, String>>,\n1262\t    ) -> Result<ProviderConfig> {\n1263\t        match provider_name {\n1264\t            \"opencode\" => {\n1265\t                let mut config = OpencodeConfig {\n1266\t                    binary_path: binary_path.to_string(),\n1267\t                    model: model.to_string(),\n1268\t                    ..Default::default()\n1269\t                };\n1270\t                if let Some(aliases) = model_aliases {\n1271\t                    config.model_aliases = aliases;\n1272\t                }\n1273\t                info!(\"ChatProviderFactory: selected opencode provider\");\n1274\t                Ok(ProviderConfig::Opencode(config))\n1275\t            }\n1276\t            \"claude-cli\" | \"\" => {\n1277\t                let config = ClaudeCliConfig {\n1278\t                    binary_path: binary_path.to_string(),\n1279\t                    model: model.to_string(),\n1280\t                    extra_args,\n1281\t                    ..Default::default()\n1282\t                };\n1283\t                info!(\"ChatProviderFactory: selected claude-cli provider\");\n1284\t                Ok(ProviderConfig::ClaudeCli(config))\n1285\t            }\n1286\t            unknown => {\n1287\t                let msg = format!(\n1288\t                    \"Unknown provider '{}'. Valid values: claude-cli, opencode.\",\n1289\t                    unknown\n1290\t                );\n1291\t                error!(\"{}\", msg);\n1292\t                Err(ChatError::ConfigError(msg))\n1293\t            }\n1294\t        }\n1295\t    }\n1296\t}\n1297\t\n1298\t// ============ Helper Functions ============\n1299\t\n1300\t// Helper functions and API test environment, you would mock the subprocess\n1534\t        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n1535\t        let tools: Vec<ProviderTool> = vec![];\n1536\t\n1537\t        // This will attempt to spawn the real claude-cli process\n1538\t        // In production tests, this should be mocked\n1539\t        let result = provider.process(\"Hello\", &context, &tools).await;\n1540\t\n1541\t        // We expect this to fail in test environment without claude-cli\n1542\t        // But we can verify the error type\n1543\t        match result {\n1544\t        // Make second call\n1576\t        let response2 = provider\n1577\t            .process(\"Second prompt\", &context, &[])\n1578\t            .await\n1579\t            .unwrap();\n1580\t        assert_eq!(response2.text, \"Response 1\"); // Same response reused\n1581\t\n1582\t        // Check call tracking\n1583\t        assert_eq!(provider.call_count().await, 2);\n1584\t\n1585\t        let
