}\n2076\t}\n2077\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d399c59f-9c5a-4e67-b699-47dd1c5bb27d","uuid":"849d50fc-8489-49f4-8eb8-5ff2bec63eba","timestamp":"2026-05-04T08:58:11.707Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/FORGE/crates/forge-chat/src/provider.rs","content":"//! Pluggable chat provider trait and implementations.\n//!\n//! This module defines the [`ChatProvider`] trait which abstracts different\n//! AI backends (CLI tools, HTTP APIs, etc.) for the chat system.\n//!\n//! ## Implementations\n//!\n//! - [`ClaudeApiProvider`] - Direct Anthropic API via HTTP (reqwest) (see [`claude_api`])\n//! - [`ClaudeCliProvider`] - claude-cli via stdin/stdout (tokio::process)\n//! - [`MockProvider`] - Testing mock that returns predefined responses\n//!\n//! ## Example\n//!\n//! ```no_run\n//! use forge_chat::{provider::{ChatProvider}, config::ClaudeApiConfig, context::DashboardContext};\n//! use forge_chat::claude_api::ClaudeApiProvider;\n//!\n//! # async fn example() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n//! let config = ClaudeApiConfig::default();\n//! let provider = ClaudeApiProvider::from_config(config)?;\n//!\n//! let context = DashboardContext::default();\n//! let tools = vec![];\n//! let response = provider.process(\"Hello!\", &context, &tools).await?;\n//! println!(\"{}\", response.text);\n//! # Ok(())\n//! # }\n//! ```\n\nuse crate::claude_api::ClaudeApiProvider;\n\nuse ::async_trait::async_trait;\nuse chrono::Utc;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::process::Stdio;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::process::Command;\nuse tokio::sync::Mutex;\nuse tokio::time::{Duration, timeout};\nuse tracing::{debug, error, info};\n\nuse crate::config::{ChatConfig, ClaudeApiConfig, ClaudeCliConfig, MockConfig, OpencodeConfig, ProviderConfig};\nuse crate::context::DashboardContext;\nuse crate::error::{ChatError, Result};\nuse crate::tools::{ToolCall, ToolDefinition};\n\n/// Response from a chat provider.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ProviderResponse {\n    /// Response text from the AI.\n    pub text: String,\n\n    /// Tool calls made during processing.\n    pub tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>,\n\n    /// Duration in milliseconds.\n    pub duration_ms: u64,\n\n    /// Estimated cost of this interaction (optional).\n    pub cost_usd: Option<f64>,\n\n    /// Reason why the response ended.\n    pub finish_reason: FinishReason,\n\n    /// Token usage information (if available).\n    pub usage: Option<TokenUsage>,\n}\n\nimpl ProviderResponse {\n    /// Create a new basic response.\n    pub fn new(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            text: text.into(),\n            tool_calls: vec![],\n            duration_ms: 0,\n            cost_usd: None,\n            finish_reason: FinishReason::Stop,\n            usage: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Add tool calls to the response.\n    pub fn with_tool_calls(mut self, calls: Vec<ToolCall>) -> Self {\n        self.tool_calls = calls;\n        self.finish_reason = if !self.tool_calls.is_empty() {\n            FinishReason::ToolCall\n        } else {\n            FinishReason::Stop\n        };\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the finish reason.\n    pub fn with_finish_reason(mut self, reason: FinishReason) -> Self {\n        self.finish_reason = reason;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the token usage.\n    pub fn with_usage(mut self, usage: TokenUsage) -> Self {\n        self.usage = Some(usage);\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the duration.\n    pub fn with_duration(mut self, duration_ms: u64) -> Self {\n        self.duration_ms = duration_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the cost.\n    pub fn with_cost(mut self, cost: f64) -> Self {\n        self.cost_usd = Some(cost);\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// Reason why the provider's response ended.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\npub enum FinishReason {\n    /// Normal stop sequence.\n    #[default]\n    Stop,\n    /// Tool calls were made.\n    ToolCall,\n    /// Max tokens reached.\n    MaxTokens,\n    /// Error occurred.\n    Error(String),\n}\n\n/// Token usage information for a provider response.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TokenUsage {\n    /// Input tokens consumed.\n    pub input_tokens: u32,\n\n    /// Output tokens consumed.\n    pub output_tokens: u32,\n\n    /// Cache read tokens (prompt caching hits).\n    pub cache_read_tokens: u32,\n\n    /// Cache creation tokens (new cache entries).\n    pub cache_creation_tokens: u32,\n}\n\nimpl TokenUsage {\n    /// Total tokens consumed (input + output).\n    pub fn total_tokens(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.input_tokens as u64 + self.output_tokens as u64\n    }\n\n    /// Total cache tokens (read + creation).\n    pub fn total_cache_tokens(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.cache_read_tokens as u64 + self.cache_creation_tokens as u64\n    }\n\n    /// Create zero usage.\n    pub fn zero() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            input_tokens: 0,\n            output_tokens: 0,\n            cache_read_tokens: 0,\n            cache_creation_tokens: 0,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for TokenUsage {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::zero()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Tool definition passed to providers.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ProviderTool {\n    /// Tool name.\n    pub name: String,\n\n    /// Tool description.\n    pub description: String,\n\n    /// Input schema (JSON Schema).\n    pub input_schema: serde_json::Value,\n}\n\nimpl From<ToolDefinition> for ProviderTool {\n    fn from(tool: ToolDefinition) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            name: tool.name,\n            description: tool.description,\n            input_schema: tool.input_schema,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Trait for AI chat providers.\n///\n/// Implementations can use different backends:\n/// - HTTP APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.)\n/// - CLI tools (claude-cli, aider, etc.)\n/// - Mock providers for testing\n#[async_trait]\npub trait ChatProvider: Send + Sync {\n    /// Process a prompt with context and tools.\n    ///\n    /// The provider may invoke tools during processing. Tool results are\n    /// NOT included in the response - callers must handle tool execution\n    /// separately based on the returned tool_calls.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `prompt` - The user's prompt/command\n    /// * `context` - Current dashboard context for contextual awareness\n    /// * `tools` - Available tools that the provider may invoke\n    async fn process(\n        &self,\n        prompt: &str,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n        tools: &[ProviderTool],\n    ) -> Result<ProviderResponse>;\n\n    /// Get the provider name for logging/debugging.\n    fn name(&self) -> &str;\n\n    /// Whether this provider supports streaming responses.\n    fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Estimated cost per request (if known).\n    ///\n    /// Returns None if cost estimation is not available for this provider.\n    /// This is useful for cost tracking and budget management.\n    fn estimated_cost(&self) -> Option<f64> {\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// Get the model name being used.\n    fn model(&self) -> &str;\n}\n\n// ============ Claude CLI Provider ============\n// Note: Claude API provider has been moved to claude_api.rs module\n\n/// Claude CLI provider using stdin/stdout communication.\n///\n/// This provider spawns a claude-cli process and communicates via JSON messages.\npub struct ClaudeCliProvider {\n    config: ClaudeCliConfig,\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    process: Mutex<Option<CliProcess>>,\n}\n\n/// Represents a running claude-cli process.\nstruct CliProcess {\n    child: tokio::process::Child,\n    stdin: tokio::process::ChildStdin,\n    stdout: tokio::process::ChildStdout,\n    stderr: tokio::process::ChildStderr,\n}\n\nimpl ClaudeCliProvider {\n    /// Create a new Claude CLI provider.\n    pub fn new(config: ClaudeCliConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            process: Mutex::new(None),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a provider from a ChatConfig (deprecated - use ProviderConfig directly).\n    #[deprecated(note = \"Use ProviderConfig::ClaudeCli directly instead\")]\n    pub fn from_chat_config(_config: &ChatConfig) -> Self {\n        Self::new(ClaudeCliConfig::default())\n    }\n\n    /// Spawn the claude-cli process.\n    async fn spawn_process(&self) -> Result<CliProcess> {\n        let mut cmd = Command::new(&self.config.binary_path);\n\n        // Add model flag\n        cmd.arg(\"--model\").arg(&self.config.model);\n\n        // Add headless flag if configured\n        if self.config.headless {\n            cmd.arg(\"--headless\");\n        }\n\n        // Add extra args\n        cmd.args(&self.config.extra_args);\n\n        // Set up stdin/stdout/stderr for JSON communication\n        cmd.stdin(Stdio::piped())\n            .stdout(Stdio::piped())\n            .stderr(Stdio::piped());\n\n        info!(\"Spawning claude-cli: {:?}\", cmd);\n\n        let mut child = cmd\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| ChatError::ConfigError(format!(\"Failed to spawn claude-cli: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let stdin = child.stdin.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stdin for claude-cli\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        let stdout = child.stdout.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stdout for claude-cli\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        let stderr = child.stderr.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stderr for claude-cli\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(CliProcess {\n            child,\n            stdin,\n            stdout,\n            stderr,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to claude-cli and read the response.\n    async fn send_request(&self, prompt: &str, tools: &[ProviderTool]) -> Result<CliResponse> {\n        // Spawn a new process for each request (simpler reliability)\n        let proc = self.spawn_process().await?;\n\n        let CliProcess {\n            mut child,\n            mut stdin,\n            stdout,\n            mut stderr,\n        } = proc;\n\n        // Build the request JSON\n        let request = CliRequest {\n            prompt: prompt.to_string(),\n            tools: tools.to_vec(),\n        };\n\n        let request_json = serde_json::to_string(&request).map_err(ChatError::JsonError)?;\n\n        // Send request\n        use tokio::io::AsyncWriteExt;\n        stdin\n            .write_all(request_json.as_bytes())\n            .await\n            .map_err(ChatError::IoError)?;\n        stdin.write_all(b\"\\n\").await.map_err(ChatError::IoError)?;\n        stdin.shutdown().await.map_err(ChatError::IoError)?;\n\n        // Read response with timeout\n        let response_bytes = timeout(Duration::from_secs(30), async {\n            use tokio::io::AsyncBufReadExt;\n            let mut reader = tokio::io::BufReader::new(stdout);\n            let mut line = String::new();\n            reader.read_line(&mut line).await?;\n            Ok::<Vec<u8>, ChatError>(line.into_bytes())\n        })\n        .await\n        .map_err(|_| {\n            // Kill the child process on timeout\n            drop(child.kill());\n            ChatError::ApiError(\"claude-cli timeout\".to_string())\n        })??;\n\n        // Also capture stderr for debugging\n        let stderr_output = timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), async {\n            use tokio::io::AsyncReadExt;\n            let mut buf = vec![];\n            stderr.read_to_end(&mut buf).await?;\n            Ok::<Vec<u8>, ChatError>(buf)\n        })\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or(Ok(vec![]))?;\n\n        if !stderr_output.is_empty() {\n            let stderr_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&stderr_output);\n            debug!(\"claude-cli stderr: {}\", stderr_str);\n        }\n\n        // Wait for process to exit\n        let status = timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), child.wait())\n            .await\n            .map_err(|_| ChatError::ApiError(\"claude-cli hang on exit\".to_string()))??;\n\n        if !status.success() {\n            return Err(ChatError::ApiError(format!(\n                \"claude-cli exited with status: {:?}\",\n                status\n            )));\n        }\n\n        // Parse response\n        let response_str = String::from_utf8(response_bytes)\n            .map_err(|e| ChatError::ApiError(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8 from claude-cli: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        debug!(\"claude-cli response: {}\", response_str);\n\n        let response: CliResponse = serde_json::from_str(&response_str).map_err(|e| {\n            ChatError::ApiError(format!(\"Failed to parse claude-cli response: {}\", e))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(response)\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatProvider for ClaudeCliProvider {\n    async fn process(\n        &self,\n        prompt: &str,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n        tools: &[ProviderTool],\n    ) -> Result<ProviderResponse> {\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n\n        // Build the enhanced prompt with context\n        let enhanced_prompt = format!(\n            \"{}\\n\\nCurrent dashboard state:\\n{}\",\n            prompt,\n            context.to_summary()\n        );\n\n        let cli_response = self.send_request(&enhanced_prompt, tools).await?;\n\n        let duration = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n\n        // Convert tool calls\n        let tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall> = cli_response\n            .tool_calls\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|tc| ToolCall {\n                name: tc.name,\n                parameters: tc.parameters,\n                id: tc.id,\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let finish_reason = if !tool_calls.is_empty() {\n            FinishReason::ToolCall\n        } else {\n            FinishReason::Stop\n        };\n\n        Ok(ProviderResponse {\n            text: cli_response.text,\n            tool_calls,\n            duration_ms: duration,\n            cost_usd: Some(0.0), // CLI provider doesn't report cost\n            finish_reason,\n            usage: None, // CLI doesn't provide token usage\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"claude-cli\"\n    }\n\n    fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n        false\n    }\n\n    fn model(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.config.model\n    }\n}\n\n/// Request sent to claude-cli.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\nstruct CliRequest {\n    prompt: String,\n    tools: Vec<ProviderTool>,\n}\n\n/// Response received from claude-cli.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\nstruct CliResponse {\n    text: String,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    tool_calls: Vec<CliToolCall>,\n}\n\n/// Tool call from claude-cli.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\nstruct CliToolCall {\n    name: String,\n    parameters: serde_json::Value,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    id: Option<String>,\n}\n\n// ============ Mock Provider ============\n\n/// Mock provider for testing.\n///\n/// Returns predefined responses without making any external calls.\n///\n/// # Features\n///\n/// - Call tracking: records all prompts sent to the provider\n/// - Multiple responses: can return different responses for sequential calls\n/// - Tool call simulation: can simulate tool calls in responses\n/// - Error simulation: can simulate errors for testing error handling\n///\n/// # Example\n///\n/// ```no_run\n/// use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n///\n/// let provider = MockProvider::new()\n///     .with_response(\"First response\")\n///     .and_then_response(\"Second response\")\n///     .and_then_error(\"API error\");\n///\n/// // First call returns \"First response\"\n/// // Second call returns \"Second response\"\n/// // Third call returns an error\n/// ```\npub struct MockProvider {\n    model: String,\n    responses: Arc<Mutex<Vec<MockResponse>>>,\n    calls: Arc<Mutex<Vec<MockCall>>>,\n    response_delay_ms: u64,\n    simulate_error_after: Option<(usize, String)>,\n}\n\n/// A mock response that can be returned by the MockProvider.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct MockResponse {\n    text: String,\n    tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>,\n    is_error: bool,\n    error_message: Option<String>,\n}\n\nimpl MockResponse {\n    fn success(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            text: text.into(),\n            tool_calls: vec![],\n            is_error: false,\n            error_message: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn with_tool_call(mut self, name: impl Into<String>, parameters: serde_json::Value) -> Self {\n        self.tool_calls.push(ToolCall {\n            name: name.into(),\n            parameters,\n            id: Some(format!(\"mock-tool-{}\", self.tool_calls.len())),\n        });\n        self\n    }\n\n    fn error(message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            text: String::new(),\n            tool_calls: vec![],\n            is_error: true,\n            error_message: Some(message.into()),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Record of a call made to the MockProvider.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MockCall {\n    /// The prompt that was sent.\n    pub prompt: String,\n    /// The tools that were available.\n    pub tools_available: usize,\n    /// Timestamp of the call.\n    #[serde(skip)]\n    pub timestamp: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl MockProvider {\n    /// Create a new mock provider with default responses.\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self::from_config(MockConfig::default())\n    }\n\n    /// Create a mock provider from config.\n    pub fn from_config(config: MockConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            model: config.model,\n            responses: Arc::new(Mutex::new(vec![MockResponse::success(config.response)])),\n            calls: Arc::new(Mutex::new(vec![])),\n            response_delay_ms: config.delay_ms,\n            simulate_error_after: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Set a single response text (replaces all queued responses).\n    pub fn with_response(mut self, text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(vec![MockResponse::success(text.into())]));\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set multiple responses at once.\n    ///\n    /// # Example\n    ///\n    /// ```no_run\n    /// # use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n    /// let provider = MockProvider::new()\n    ///     .with_multiple_responses([\"First\", \"Second\", \"Third\"]);\n    /// ```\n    pub fn with_multiple_responses<I>(mut self, responses: I) -> Self\n    where\n        I: IntoIterator,\n        I::Item: Into<String>,\n    {\n        let resp: Vec<MockResponse> = responses\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|s| MockResponse::success(s.into()))\n            .collect();\n        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(resp));\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Add another response to the queue (chains with current responses).\n    ///\n    /// Note: This method may not work correctly when the provider is already\n    /// shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. For reliable multi-response setup, use\n    /// `with_multiple_responses` for initial configuration, or `add_response`\n    /// for dynamic modification after sharing.\n    ///\n    /// For modifying responses on a shared provider, use `add_response` instead.\n    pub fn and_then_response(mut self, text: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        // Clone the Arc first to avoid borrow issues\n        let responses_arc = self.responses.clone();\n        let mut current = match responses_arc.try_lock() {\n            Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Lock failed, can't chain\n                return self;\n            }\n        };\n\n        current.push(MockResponse::success(text.into()));\n        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(current));\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Add a tool call response to the queue.\n    ///\n    /// Note: This method may not work correctly when the provider is already\n    /// shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. For dynamic tool call chaining on shared\n    /// providers, use `add_tool_call_response` instead.\n    ///\n    /// For modifying responses on a shared provider, use `add_tool_call_response`.\n    pub fn and_then_tool_call(\n        mut self,\n        tool_name: impl Into<String>,\n        parameters: serde_json::Value,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let responses_arc = self.responses.clone();\n        let mut current = match responses_arc.try_lock() {\n            Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n            Err(_) => return self,\n        };\n\n        current.push(MockResponse::success(\"\").with_tool_call(tool_name, parameters));\n        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(current));\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Add an error response to the queue.\n    ///\n    /// Note: This method may not work correctly when the provider is already\n    /// shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. For dynamic error chaining on shared providers,\n    /// use `add_error` instead.\n    ///\n    /// For modifying responses on a shared provider, use `add_error`.\n    pub fn and_then_error(mut self, error_message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        let responses_arc = self.responses.clone();\n        let mut current = match responses_arc.try_lock() {\n            Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n            Err(_) => return self,\n        };\n\n        current.push(MockResponse::error(error_message));\n        self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(current));\n        self\n    }\n\n    // ============ Methods for Shared Provider (Arc<Mutex<>>) Pattern ============\n    //\n    // The following methods work on &self and can be used when the provider\n    // is shared via Arc<Mutex<MockProvider>>. Use these for dynamic response\n    // chaining after the provider has been shared.\n\n    /// Add a tool call response to the queue (works on shared references).\n    ///\n    /// Unlike `and_then_tool_call`, this method works on `&self` and can be called\n    /// even when the provider is shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. It modifies the shared\n    /// response queue directly through the mutex.\n    ///\n    /// # Example\n    ///\n    /// ```no_run\n    /// # use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n    /// # use std::sync::Arc;\n    /// # async fn example() {\n    /// let provider = Arc::new(MockProvider::new());\n    /// let provider_ref = provider.clone();\n    ///\n    /// // This works because add_tool_call_response takes &self\n    /// provider_ref.add_tool_call_response(\"tool_name\".to_string(), serde_json::json!({})).await;\n    /// # }\n    /// ```\n    pub async fn add_tool_call_response(&self, tool_name: String, parameters: serde_json::Value) {\n        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n        responses.push(MockResponse::success(\"\").with_tool_call(tool_name, parameters));\n    }\n\n    /// Add a text response to the queue (works on shared references).\n    ///\n    /// Unlike `and_then_response`, this method works on `&self` and can be called\n    /// even when the provider is shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`.\n    pub async fn add_response(&self, text: String) {\n        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n        responses.push(MockResponse::success(text));\n    }\n\n    /// Add an error response to the queue (works on shared references).\n    ///\n    /// Unlike `and_then_error`, this method works on `&self` and can be called\n    /// even when the provider is shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`.\n    pub async fn add_error(&self, error_message: String) {\n        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n        responses.push(MockResponse::error(error_message));\n    }\n\n    /// Add multiple responses to the queue (works on shared references).\n    ///\n    /// # Example\n    ///\n    /// ```no_run\n    /// # use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n    /// # async fn example() {\n    /// let provider = MockProvider::new();\n    /// provider.add_multiple_responses([\"First\", \"Second\", \"Third\"]).await;\n    /// # }\n    /// ```\n    pub async fn add_multiple_responses<I>(&self, responses: I)\n    where\n        I: IntoIterator,\n        I::Item: Into<String>,\n    {\n        let mut mock_responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n        for text in responses {\n            mock_responses.push(MockResponse::success(text.into()));\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all responses from the queue.\n    ///\n    /// This is useful when you want to start fresh without the default response.\n    ///\n    /// # Example\n    ///\n    /// ```no_run\n    /// # use forge_chat::provider::MockProvider;\n    /// # async fn example() {\n    /// let provider = MockProvider::new();\n    /// provider.clear_responses().await;\n    /// provider.add_response(\"Custom response\".to_string()).await;\n    /// # }\n    /// ```\n    pub async fn clear_responses(&self) {\n        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n        responses.clear();\n    }\n\n    /// Simulate errors after N calls.\n    pub fn with_error_after(mut self, count: usize, error_message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.simulate_error_after = Some((count, error_message.into()));\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the response delay.\n    pub fn with_delay(mut self, delay_ms: u64) -> Self {\n        self.response_delay_ms = delay_ms;\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Set the model name.\n    pub fn with_model(mut self, model: impl Into<String>) -> Self {\n        self.model = model.into();\n        self\n    }\n\n    /// Get the recorded calls.\n    pub async fn get_calls(&self) -> Vec<MockCall> {\n        self.calls.lock().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of calls made.\n    pub async fn call_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.calls.lock().await.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Clear the call history.\n    pub async fn clear_calls(&self) {\n        self.calls.lock().await.clear();\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a specific prompt was called.\n    pub async fn was_called_with(&self, prompt: &str) -> bool {\n        let calls = self.calls.lock().await;\n        calls.iter().any(|c| c.prompt.contains(prompt))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for MockProvider {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatProvider for MockProvider {\n    async fn process(\n        &self,\n        prompt: &str,\n        _context: &DashboardContext,\n        tools: &[ProviderTool],\n    ) -> Result<ProviderResponse> {\n        // Record the call\n        let call = MockCall {\n            prompt: prompt.to_string(),\n            tools_available: tools.len(),\n            timestamp: Utc::now(),\n        };\n        self.calls.lock().await.push(call);\n\n        // Check if we should simulate an error\n        if let Some((count, ref error_msg)) = self.simulate_error_after {\n            let current_count = self.calls.lock().await.len();\n            if current_count > count {\n                return Err(ChatError::ApiError(error_msg.clone()));\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Simulate delay\n        if self.response_delay_ms > 0 {\n            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(self.response_delay_ms)).await;\n        }\n\n        // Get the next response\n        let mut responses = self.responses.lock().await;\n        let response = if responses.is_empty() {\n            // If no responses queued, return a default\n            MockResponse::success(\"Mock response\")\n        } else if responses.len() == 1 {\n            // If only one response, reuse it (for single-response providers)\n            responses[0].clone()\n        } else {\n            // If multiple responses, consume one and return it\n            responses.remove(0)\n        };\n\n        // Check if this is an error response\n        if response.is_error {\n            return Err(ChatError::ApiError(\n                response\n                    .error_message\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|| \"Mock error\".to_string()),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        Ok(ProviderResponse {\n            text: response.text,\n            tool_calls: response.tool_calls,\n            duration_ms: self.response_delay_ms,\n            cost_usd: Some(0.0),\n            finish_reason: FinishReason::Stop,\n            usage: None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"mock\"\n    }\n\n    fn estimated_cost(&self) -> Option<f64> {\n        Some(0.0)\n    }\n\n    fn model(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.model\n    }\n}\n\n// ============ Opencode CLI Provider ============\n\n/// Opencode CLI provider using subprocess communication.\n///\n/// This provider spawns an opencode process with `--format json` and parses\n/// the streaming JSON output events.\n///\n/// # Output Format\n///\n/// Opencode outputs JSON lines with the following event types:\n/// - `step_start`: Indicates a new step is starting\n/// - `text`: Contains actual text output in `part.text`\n/// - `step_finish`: Indicates step completion with token usage\npub struct OpencodeProvider {\n    config: OpencodeConfig,\n}\n\nimpl OpencodeProvider {\n    /// Create a new Opencode provider.\n    pub fn new(config: OpencodeConfig) -> Self {\n        Self { config }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a provider from a ChatConfig.\n    pub fn from_chat_config(_config: &ChatConfig) -> Self {\n        Self::new(OpencodeConfig::default())\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to opencode and parse the streaming JSON response.\n    async fn send_request(&self, prompt: &str) -> Result<OpencodeResponse> {\n        let mut cmd = Command::new(&self.config.binary_path);\n\n        // Build command: opencode run --format json --model <model> \"<prompt>\"\n        cmd.args([\"run\", \"--format\", \"json\", \"--model\", &self.config.model])\n            .arg(prompt);\n\n        // Set up pipes\n        cmd.stdin(Stdio::null())\n            .stdout(Stdio::piped())\n            .stderr(Stdio::piped());\n\n        info!(\"Spawning opencode: {:?}\", cmd);\n\n        let mut child = cmd\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| ChatError::ConfigError(format!(\"Failed to spawn opencode: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        let stdout = child.stdout.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stdout for opencode\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        let stderr = child.stderr.take().ok_or_else(|| {\n            ChatError::ConfigError(\"Failed to open stderr for opencode\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        // Read stdout with timeout\n        let stdout_bytes = timeout(Duration::from_secs(self.config.timeout_secs), async {\n            use tokio::io::AsyncReadExt;\n            let mut buf = vec![];\n            let mut stdout = stdout;\n            stdout.read_to_end(&mut buf).await?;\n            Ok::<Vec<u8>, std::io::Error>(buf)\n        })\n        .await\n        .map_err(|_| {\n            drop(child.kill());\n            ChatError::ApiError(\"opencode timeout\".to_string())\n        })?\n        .map_err(ChatError::IoError)?;\n\n        // Read stderr for debugging\n        let stderr_bytes = timeout(Duration::from_secs(1), async {\n            use tokio::io::AsyncReadExt;\n            let mut buf = vec![];\n            let mut stderr = stderr;\n            stderr.read_to_end(&mut buf).await?;\n            Ok::<Vec<u8>, std::io::Error>(buf)\n        })\n        .await\n        .unwrap_or(Ok(vec![]))\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n\n        // Wait for process to exit\n        let status = timeout(Duration::from_secs(5), child.wait())\n            .await\n            .map_err(|_| ChatError::ApiError(\"opencode hang on exit\".to_string()))??;\n\n        if !status.success() {\n            let stderr_str = String::from_utf8_lossy(&stderr_bytes);\n            return Err(ChatError::ApiError(format!(\n                \"opencode exited with status: {:?}, stderr: {}\",\n                status, stderr_str\n            )));\n        }\n\n        // Parse the streaming JSON response\n        let stdout_str = String::from_utf8(stdout_bytes)\n            .map_err(|e| ChatError::ApiError(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8 from opencode: {}\", e)))?;\n\n        debug!(\"opencode raw output: {}\", stdout_str);\n\n        self.parse_opencode_response(&stdout_str)\n    }\n\n    /// Parse opencode streaming JSON response.\n    ///\n    /// The response consists of JSON lines with events:\n    /// - {\"type\":\"step_start\",...}\n    /// - {\"type\":\"text\",\"part\":{\"text\":\"Hello there\"},...}\n    /// - {\"type\":\"step_finish\",\"part\":{\"tokens\":{...},\"cost\":0},...}\n    fn parse_opencode_response(&self, output: &str) -> Result<OpencodeResponse> {\n        let mut text_parts = Vec::new();\n        let mut total_cost = 0.0;\n        let mut usage = TokenUsage::zero();\n\n        for line in output.lines() {\n            let line = line.trim();\n            if line.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Parse each JSON line\n            let event: OpencodeEvent = match serde_json::from_str(line) {\n                Ok(e) => e,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    debug!(\"Failed to parse opencode line as event: {} - {}\", line, e);\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            match event.event_type.as_str() {\n                \"text\" => {\n                    // Extract text from the part.text field\n                    if let Some(part) = event.part\n                        && let Some(text) = part.text\n                        && part.synthetic != Some(true)\n                    {\n                        // Skip synthetic messages (like \"Continue if you have next steps\")\n                        text_parts.push(text);\n                    }\n                }\n                \"step_finish\" => {\n                    // Extract cost and token usage\n                    if let Some(part) = event.part {\n                        if let Some(cost) = part.cost {\n                            total_cost += cost;\n                        }\n                        if let Some(tokens) = part.tokens {\n                            usage.input_tokens = tokens.input.unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n                            usage.output_tokens = tokens.output.unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    // Ignore step_start and other events\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Combine all text parts\n        let full_text = text_parts.join(\"\\n\");\n\n        Ok(OpencodeResponse {\n            text: full_text,\n            cost: total_cost,\n            usage,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Response from opencode CLI.\nstruct OpencodeResponse {\n    text: String,\n    cost: f64,\n    usage: TokenUsage,\n}\n\n/// Opencode streaming event types.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\nstruct OpencodeEvent {\n    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n    event_type: String,\n    part: Option<OpencodePart>,\n}\n\n/// Opencode event part data.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\nstruct OpencodePart {\n    text: Option<String>,\n    #[serde(default)]\n    synthetic: Option<bool>,\n    cost: Option<f64>,\n    tokens: Option<OpencodeTokens>,\n}\n\n/// Token usage from opencode.\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\nstruct OpencodeTokens {\n    input: Option<u64>,\n    output: Option<u64>,\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ChatProvider for OpencodeProvider {\n    async fn process(\n        &self,\n        prompt: &str,\n        context: &DashboardContext,\n        _tools: &[ProviderTool],\n    ) -> Result<ProviderResponse> {\n        let start = std::time::Instant::now();\n\n        // Build the enhanced prompt with context\n        let enhanced_prompt = format!(\n            \"{}\\n\\nCurrent dashboard state:\\n{}\",\n            prompt,\n            context.to_summary()\n        );\n\n        let response = self.send_request(&enhanced_prompt).await?;\n\n        let duration = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n\n        Ok(ProviderResponse {\n            text: response.text,\n            tool_calls: vec![],\n            duration_ms: duration,\n            cost_usd: Some(response.cost),\n            finish_reason: FinishReason::Stop,\n            usage: Some(response.usage),\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"opencode\"\n    }\n\n    fn supports_streaming(&self) -> bool {\n        false\n    }\n\n    fn model(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.config.model\n    }\n}\n\n// ============ Provider Factory ============\n\n/// Create a provider from configuration.\n///\n/// This factory function creates the appropriate provider based on the\n/// `ProviderConfig` in `ChatConfig`.\n///\n/// # Example\n///\n/// ```no_run\n/// use forge_chat::{ChatConfig, provider::create_provider};\n///\n/// # fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n/// let config = ChatConfig::default();\n/// let provider = create_provider(&config)?;\n/// # Ok(())\n/// # }\n/// ```\npub fn create_provider(config: &ChatConfig) -> Result<Box<dyn ChatProvider>> {\n    // Check for environment variable override (for backward compatibility)\n    if let Ok(provider_type) = std::env::var(\"FORGE_CHAT_PROVIDER\") {\n        info!(\n            \"Using FORGE_CHAT_PROVIDER environment variable: {}\",\n            provider_type\n        );\n        return create_provider_by_type(&provider_type, config);\n    }\n\n    // Use the provider from config\n    match &config.provider {\n        ProviderConfig::ClaudeApi(api_config) => {\n            info!(\"Creating claude-api provider\");\n            Ok(Box::new(ClaudeApiProvider::from_config(\n                api_config.clone(),\n            )?))\n        }\n        ProviderConfig::ClaudeCli(cli_config) => {\n            info!(\"Creating claude-cli provider\");\n            Ok(Box::new(ClaudeCliProvider::new(cli_config.clone())))\n        }\n        ProviderConfig::Opencode(opencode_config) => {\n            info!(\"Creating opencode provider\");\n            Ok(Box::new(OpencodeProvider::new(opencode_config.clone())))\n        }\n        ProviderConfig::Mock(mock_config) => {\n            info!(\"Creating mock provider\");\n            Ok(Box::new(MockProvider::from_config(mock_config.clone())))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Create a provider by type string (for backward compatibility with env var).\nfn create_provider_by_type(\n    provider_type: &str,\n    _config: &ChatConfig,\n) -> Result<Box<dyn ChatProvider>> {\n    match provider_type {\n        \"claude-cli\" => {\n            info!(\"Creating claude-cli provider from env var\");\n            Ok(Box::new(ClaudeCliProvider::new(ClaudeCliConfig::default())))\n        }\n        \"opencode\" => {\n            info!(\"Creating opencode provider from env var\");\n            Ok(Box::new(OpencodeProvider::new(OpencodeConfig::default())))\n        }\n        \"mock\" => {\n            info!(\"Creating mock provider from env var\");\n            Ok(Box::new(MockProvider::new()))\n        }\n        _ => {\n            info!(\"Creating claude-api provider from env var\");\n            Ok(Box::new(ClaudeApiProvider::from_config(\n                ClaudeApiConfig::default(),\n            )?))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Factory for creating chat providers based on a provider name string.\n///\n/// Reads the `provider` field from configuration and instantiates the appropriate\n/// [`ProviderConfig`]. Defaults to `\"claude-cli\"` for backwards compatibility.\n///\n/// # Example\n///\n/// ```no_run\n/// use forge_chat::provider::ChatProviderFactory;\n///\n/// # fn main() -> forge_chat::error::Result<()> {\n/// let config = ChatProviderFactory::create_config(\"opencode\", \"opencode\", \"sonnet\", vec![], None)?;\n/// # Ok(())\n/// # }\n/// ```\npub struct ChatProviderFactory;\n\nimpl ChatProviderFactory {\n    /// Create a [`ProviderConfig`] from a provider name string.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// - `provider_name` — `\"opencode\"` or `\"claude-cli\"`. An empty string falls\n    ///   back to `\"claude-cli\"` for backwards compatibility.\n    /// - `binary_path` — Path/name of the provider binary.\n    /// - `model` — Model identifier to use.\n    /// - `extra_args` — Additional CLI arguments (used by `claude-cli`).\n    /// - `model_aliases` — Model alias map (used by `opencode`).\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    ///\n    /// Returns [`ChatError::ConfigError`] if `provider_name` is not recognised.\n    pub fn create_config(\n        provider_name: &str,\n        binary_path: &str,\n        model: &str,\n        extra_args: Vec<String>,\n        model_aliases: Option<std::collections::HashMap<String, String>>,\n    ) -> Result<ProviderConfig> {\n        match provider_name {\n            \"opencode\" => {\n                let mut config = OpencodeConfig {\n                    binary_path: binary_path.to_string(),\n                    model: model.to_string(),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                };\n                if let Some(aliases) = model_aliases {\n                    config.model_aliases = aliases;\n                }\n                info!(\"ChatProviderFactory: selected opencode provider\");\n                Ok(ProviderConfig::Opencode(config))\n            }\n            \"claude-cli\" | \"\" => {\n                let config = ClaudeCliConfig {\n                    binary_path: binary_path.to_string(),\n                    model: model.to_string(),\n                    extra_args,\n                    ..Default::default()\n                };\n                info!(\"ChatProviderFactory: selected claude-cli provider\");\n                Ok(ProviderConfig::ClaudeCli(config))\n            }\n            unknown => {\n                let msg = format!(\n                    \"Unknown provider '{}'. Valid values: claude-cli, opencode.\",\n                    unknown\n                );\n                error!(\"{}\", msg);\n                Err(ChatError::ConfigError(msg))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ============ Helper Functions ============\n\n// Helper functions and API types have been moved to claude_api and claude_api_types modules\n// See claude_api.rs for SYSTEM_PROMPT and provider implementation\n// See claude_api_types.rs for ApiRequest, ApiResponse, ContentBlock, etc.\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider() {\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        let provider = MockProvider::new()\n            .with_response(\"Test response\")\n            .with_delay(10);\n\n        assert_eq!(provider.name(), \"mock\");\n        assert_eq!(provider.model(), \"mock-model\");\n        assert!(!provider.supports_streaming());\n        assert_eq!(provider.estimated_cost(), Some(0.0));\n\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n        let response = provider.process(\"Hello\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(response.text, \"Test response\");\n        assert_eq!(response.tool_calls.len(), 0);\n        assert!(response.duration_ms >= 10);\n        assert_eq!(response.cost_usd, Some(0.0));\n        assert_eq!(response.finish_reason, FinishReason::Stop);\n        assert!(response.usage.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_claude_cli_config_default() {\n        let config = assert!(config.extra_args.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_provider_tool_conversion() {\n        let tool_def = ToolDefinition {\n            name: \"test_tool\".to_string(),\n #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_call_tracking() {\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        let provider = MockProvider::new().with_response(\"Response 1\");\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        // Make first call\n        let response1 = provider\n            .process(\"First prompt\", &context, &[])\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(response1.text, \"Response 1\");\n\n        // Make second call\n        let response2 = provider\n            .process(\"Second prompt\", &context, &[])\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(response2.text, \"Response 1\"); // Same response reused\n\n        // Check call tracking\n        assert_eq!(provider.call_count().await, 2);\n\n        let calls = provider.get_calls().await;\n        assert_eq!(calls.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(calls[0].prompt, \"First prompt\");\n        assert_eq!(calls[1].prompt, \"Second prompt\");\n        assert!(provider.was_called_with(\"First prompt\").await);\n        assert!(provider.was_called_with(\"Second prompt\").await);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_multiple_responses() {\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        // Use with_multiple_responses for reliable chaining\n        let provider = MockProvider::new().with_multiple_responses([\"First\", \"Second\", \"Third\"]);\n\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        let r1 = provider.process(\"test\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r1.text, \"First\");\n\n        let r2 = provider.process(\"test\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r2.text, \"Second\");\n\n        let r3 = provider.process(\"test\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r3.text, \"Third\");\n\n        // Fourth call reuses the last response (when only 1 remains, it's reused)\n        let r4 = provider.process(\"test\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r4.text, \"Third\"); // Last response is reused\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn succeed\n        assert!(provider.process(\"test\", &context, &[]).await.is_ok());\n        assert!(provider.process(\"test\", &context, &[]).await.is_ok());\n\n        // Third call fails\n assert!(msg.contains(\"Error after 2 calls\"));\n            }\n            _ => panic!(\"Expected ApiError\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_clear_calls() {\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        let provider = MockProvider::new();\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        // Make some calls\n        provider.process(\"test1\", &context, &[]).await.ok();\n        provider.process(\"test2\", &context, &[]).await.ok();\n\n        assert_eq!(provider.call_count().await, 2);\n\n        // Clear calls\n        provider.clear_calls().await;\n        assert_eq!(provider.call_count().await, 0);\n\n        // New call adds to the cleared list\n        provider.process(\"test3\", &context, &[]).await.ok();\n        assert_eq!(provider.call_count().await, 1);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_from_config() {\n        use crate::config::MockConfig;\n\n r#\"{\"type\":\"text\",\"timestamp\":1772352998964,\"sessionID\":\"ses_123\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"prt_123\",\"sessionID\":\"ses_123\",\"messageID\":\"msg_123\",\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Hello there\",\"time\":{\"start\":1772352998958,\"end\":1772352998958}}}\"#;\n\n        let event: OpencodeEvent = serde_json::from_str(text_event_json).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(event.event_type, \"text\");\n        assert!(event.part.is_some());\n        let part = event.part.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(part.text, Some(\"Hello there\".to_string()));\n        assert_eq!(part.synthetic, None);\n\n        // Test parsing a step_finish event with tokens\n        let finish_event_json = r#\"{\"type\":\"step_finish\",\"timestamp\":1772352999343,\"sessionID\":\"ses_123\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"prt_123\",\"sessionID\":\"ses_123\",\"messageID\":\"msg_123\",\"type\":\"step-finish\",\"reason\":\"stop\",\"cost\":0.001,\"tokens\":{\"input\":100,\"output\":50,\"reasoning\":0,\"cache\":{\"read\":0,\"write\":0}}}}\"#;\n\n        let finish_event: OpencodeEvent = serde_json::from_str(finish_event_json).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(finish_event.event_type, \"step_finish\");\n        let part = finish_event.part.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(part.cost, Some(0.001));\n        assert!(part.tokens.is_some());\n        let tokens = part.tokens.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(tokens.input, Some(100));\n        assert_eq!(tokens.output, Some(50));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_opencode_synthetic_message_filtering() {\n        // Synthetic messages should be filtered out\n        let synthetic_event_json = r#\"{\"type\":\"text\",\"timestamp\":1772353020961,\"sessionID\":\"ses_123\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"prt_123\",\"sessionID\":\"ses_123\",\"messageID\":\"msg_123\",\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Continue if you have next steps\",\"synthetic\":true,\"time\":{\"start\":1772353020960,\"end\":1772353020960}}}\"#;\n\n        let event: OpencodeEvent = serde_json::from_str(synthetic_event_json).unwrap();\n        let part = event.part.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(part.synthetic, Some(true));\n        assert_eq!(part.text, Some(\"Continue if you have next steps\".to_string()));\n\n        // Non-synthetic messages should not be filtered\n        let normal_event_json = r#\"{\"type\":\"text\",\"timestamp\":1772353020961,\"sessionID\":\"ses_123\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"prt_123\",\"sessionID\":\"ses_123\",\"messageID\":\"msg_123\",\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Normal response\",\"time\":{\"start\":1772353020960,\"end\":1772353020960}}}\"#;\n\n        let event: OpencodeEvent = serde_json::from_str(normal_event_json).unwrap();\n        let part = event.part.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(part.synthetic, None);\n        assert_eq!(part.text, Some(\"Normal response\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn assert_eq!(response.usage.input_tokens, 100);\n        assert_eq!(response.usage.output_tokens, 50);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_opencode_response_parsing_with_multiple_text_events() {\n        // Test parsing output with multiple text events\n        let output = r#\"{\"type\":\"step_start\",\"timestamp\":1,\"sessionID\":\"ses_1\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"p1\",\"type\":\"step-start\"}}\n{\"type\":\"text\",\"timestamp\":2,\"sessionID\":\"ses_1\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"p2\",\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"First part\"}}\n{\"type\":\"text\",\"timestamp\":3,\"sessionID\":\"ses_1\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"p3\",\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Second part\"}}\n{\"type\":\"step_finish\",\"timestamp\":4,\"sessionID\":\"ses_1\",\"part\":{\"id\":\"p4\",\"type\":\"step-finish\",\"cost\":0.0,\"tokens\":{\"input\":200,\"output\":100}}}\"#;\n\n        let config = OpencodeConfig::default();\n        let provider = OpencodeProvider::new(config);\n\n        let response = provider.parse_opencode_response(output).unwrap();\n\n        // Multiple text events should be joined with newlines\n        assert_eq!(response.text, \"First part\\nSecond part\");\n        assert_eq!(response.usage.input_tokens, 200);\n        assert_eq!(response.usage.output_tokens, 100);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_opencode_provider_integration() {\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        let config = OpencodeConfig::default();\n        let provider = OpencodeProvider::new(config);\n\n        // Note: This test will fail if opencode is not installed\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n        let tools: Vec<ProviderTool> = vec![];\n\n        // This will attempt to spawn the real opencode process\n        let result = provider.process(\"Hello\", &context, &tools).await;\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(response) => {\n                // If opencode is installed and working, verify response\n                assert!(!response.text.is_empty() || !response.tool_calls.is_empty());\n                assert_eq!(response.finish_reason, FinishReason::Stop);\n            }\n            Err(ChatError::ConfigError(_)) => {\n                // Expected: opencode not found\n            }\n            Err(ChatError::ApiError(_)) => {\n                // Also possible: opencode failed\n            }\n            Err(other) => {\n                panic!(\"Unexpected error type: {:?}\", other);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ============ Tests for Shared Provider (Arc<Mutex<>>) Pattern ============\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_add_tool_call_response_on_shared() {\n        use std::sync::Arc;\n\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        // Create a shared provider and clear the default response\n        let provider = Arc::new(MockProvider::new());\n        provider.clear_responses().await;\n\n        let provider_clone = Arc::clone(&provider);\n\n        // Add a tool call response via the shared reference\n        provider_clone\n            .add_tool_call_response(\n                \"get_worker_status\".to_string(),\n                serde_json::json!({\"worker_id\": \"worker-1\"}),\n            )\n            .await;\n\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        // The response should be available through the original Arc\n        let response = provider.process(\"test\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(response.tool_calls.len() == 1);\n        assert_eq!(response.tool_calls[0].name, \"get_worker_status\");\n        assert_eq!(\n            response.tool_calls[0].parameters,\n            serde_json::json!({\"worker_id\": \"worker-1\"})\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_add_response_on_shared() {\n        use std::sync::Arc;\n\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        // Create a shared provider and clear the default response\n        let provider = Arc::new(MockProvider::new());\n        provider.clear_responses().await;\n\n        let provider_clone = Arc::clone(&provider);\n\n        // Add responses via the shared reference\n        provider_clone\n            .add_response(\"First response\".to_string())\n            .await;\n        provider_clone\n            .add_response(\"Second response\".to_string())\n            .await;\n\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        // Both responses should be available\n        let r1 = provider.process(\"test1\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r1.text, \"First response\");\n\n        let r2 = provider.process(\"test2\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r2.text, \"Second response\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_add_multiple_responses_on_shared() {\n        use std::sync::Arc;\n\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        // Create a shared provider and clear the default response\n        let provider = Arc::new(MockProvider::new());\n        provider.clear_responses().await;\n\n        // Add multiple responses at once\n        provider\n            .add_multiple_responses([\"First\", \"Second\", \"Third\"])\n            .await;\n\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        let r1 = provider.process(\"test1\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r1.text, \"First\");\n\n        let r2 = provider.process(\"test2\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r2.text, \"Second\");\n\n        let r3 = provider.process(\"test3\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r3.text, \"Third\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_add_error_on_shared() {\n        use std::sync::Arc;\n\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        // Create a shared provider and clear the default response\n        let provider = Arc::new(MockProvider::new());\n        provider.clear_responses().await;\n\n        provider\n            .add_response(\"Normal response\".to_string())\n            .await;\n\n        // Add an error response\n        provider\n            .add_error(\"Simulated error\".to_string())\n            .await;\n\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        // First call succeeds\n        let r1 = provider.process(\"test1\", &context, &[]).await;\n        assert!(r1.is_ok());\n        assert_eq!(r1.unwrap().text, \"Normal response\");\n\n        // Second call fails with the error\n        let r2 = provider.process(\"test2\", &context, &[]).await;\n        assert!(r2.is_err());\n        match r2 {\n            Err(ChatError::ApiError(msg)) => {\n                assert!(msg.contains(\"Simulated error\"));\n            }\n            _ => panic!(\"Expected ApiError\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_dynamic_tool_call_chaining() {\n        use std::sync::Arc;\n\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        // This test demonstrates the use case: dynamically adding tool calls\n        // after the provider has been shared (e.g., in a multi-turn conversation)\n        let provider = Arc::new(MockProvider::new());\n        provider.clear_responses().await;\n\n        // Initial state: add an initial response\n        provider\n            .add_response(\"Hello, how can I help?\".to_string())\n            .await;\n\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        // First call gets the initial response\n        let r1 = provider.process(\"query 1\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r1.text, \"Hello, how can I help?\");\n\n        // Now dynamically add tool calls (simulating a conversation flow)\n        // Note: The initial response is still in the queue (single response is reused)\n        // Adding more responses means they'll be consumed in order\n        provider\n            .add_tool_call_response(\"get_status\".to_string(), serde_json::json!({}))\n            .await;\n        provider\n            .add_response(\"Status: All systems operational\".to_string())\n            .await;\n\n        // Second call: we now have 3 responses (original + 2 new), so the first is removed\n        // The first response is still \"Hello, how can I help?\"\n        let r2 = provider.process(\"query 2\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r2.text, \"Hello, how can I help?\");\n\n        // Third call: now we have 2 responses, first is the tool call\n        let r3 = provider.process(\"query 3\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r3.tool_calls[0].name, \"get_status\");\n\n        // Fourth call: now we have 1 response left, the text response\n        let r4 = provider.process(\"query 4\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r4.text, \"Status: All systems operational\");\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_mock_provider_shared_between_multiple_tasks() {\n        use std::sync::Arc;\n\n        use crate::context::DashboardContext;\n\n        // Create a shared provider and clear the default response\n        let provider = Arc::new(MockProvider::new());\n        provider.clear_responses().await;\n\n        // Add responses from one \"task\"\n        let provider_task1 = Arc::clone(&provider);\n        provider_task1\n            .add_response(\"Response from task 1\".to_string())\n            .await;\n\n        // Add responses from another \"task\"\n        let provider_task2 = Arc::clone(&provider);\n        provider_task2\n            .add_response(\"Response from task 2\".to_string())\n            .await;\n\n        let context = DashboardContext::default();\n\n        // Both responses are available to all tasks\n        let r1 = provider.process(\"test1\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r1.text, \"Response from task 1\");\n\n        let r2 = provider.process(\"test2\", &context, &[]).await.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(r2.text, \"Response from task 2\");\n This means the `and_then_*` methods should work fine for the initial configuration of the provider before it's boxed.\n\nHowever, I still see the issue mentioned in the bead: the `and_then_tool_call` method has a problem when the provider is shared via `Arc<Mutex<>>`. The current code has:\n1. Builder methods (`and_then_*`) that work on `self` and return `Self` - these are for initial configuration\n2. Async methods (`add_*`) that work on `&self` - these are for dynamic modification after sharing via `Arc<Mutex<>>`\n\nThe bead description says there's a skipped test at line 1522, but looking at the current code, there's no such skipped test. The tests that exist (like `test_mock_provider_with_tool_calls`) use `and_then_tool_call` and they work fine.\n\nLooking at the implementation of `and_then_tool_call` more closely:\n\n```rust\npub fn and_then_tool_call(\n    mut self,\n    tool_name: impl Into<String>,\n    parameters: serde_json::Value,\n) -> Self {\n    let responses_arc = self.responses.clone();\n    let mut current = match responses_arc.try_lock() {\n        Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n        Err(_) => return self,\n    };\n\n    current.push(MockResponse::success(\"\").with_tool_call(tool_name, parameters));\n    self.responses = Arc::new(Mutex::new(current));\n    self\n}\n```\n\nThe problem here is that:\n1. It clones the Arc\n2. Tries to lock and clone the inner Vec\n3. Creates a NEW Arc<Mutex<Vec<>>>\n4. Returns self with the new Arc\n\nThis means if someone has already taken an Arc clone of the provider, they won't see the updates because a completely new Arc is created. This is a fundamental issue with the pattern.\n\n\nThe bead seems outdated since the tests are passing and there's already a working solution for shared providers. However, I should examine the git history to understand what was actually changed and what the original issue was.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d399c59f-9c5a-4e67-b699-47dd1c5bb27d","uuid":"312ddfb3-6bb5-4282-bc16-69defc44faf9"}

Ctrl+U triggers update\\n2. Cargo build succeeds\\n3. cp fails with 'Text file busy'\\n4. Error shown in TUI but update doesn't complete\\n\\n## Investigation\\n\\nSee docs/investigations/fg-2pe-ctrl-u-investigation.md for full analysis.\\n\\n## Recommended Fix\\n\\nThe self_update.rs module already has the correct approach:\\n1. Download new binary to staging location\\n2. On restart, rename staging binary to final location\\n3. Show 'Update ready - restart required' message\\n\\nThe issue is that the current running binary is at ~/.cargo/bin/forge and cannot be overwritten.\\n\\n## Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n- [ ] Update shows clear status during download/build\\n- [ ] Update completes successfully even when forge is running\\n- [ ] User sees 'Update ready - restart required' message\\n- [ ] Restart applies the update correctly\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- docs/investigations/fg-2pe-ctrl-u-investigation.md\\n- crates/forge-core/src/self_update.rs\\n- update-forge.sh\",\"design\":\"\",\"acceptance_criteria\":\"\",\"notes\":\"\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":0,\"issue_type\":\"bug\",\"assignee\":\"claude-code-glm-4.7-echo\",\"owner\":\"\",\"created_at\":\"2026-02-18T05:51:09.766674030Z\",\"created_by\":\"coder\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-28T18:29:40.936641485Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-28T18:29:40.936578096Z\",\"close_reason\":\"## Summary of work completed\\n\\nThe 'Text file busy' error when updating forge has been **fully resolved**. The fix was implemented in commits:\\n- `aec5f73` - Fixed env var propagation and staging path issues\\n- `7ffe04e` - Fixed missing should_quit to trigger restart\\n\\n## How the fix works\\n\\nThe self-update flow now correctly avoids overwriting the running binary:\\n\\n1. **Download to staging**: `perform_update()` downloads the new binary to `/tmp/forge-update/forge-staged` (not the final location)\\n2. **Trigger restart**: After successful download, `should_quit = true` is set, causing the main loop to exit\\n3. **Exec staging binary**: `restart_with_new_binary()` sets env vars (FORGE_AUTO_RESTART, FORGE_INSTALL_PATH, FORGE_STAGING_PATH) and execs the staging binary\\n4. **Self-install on startup**: The new binary detects the restart env vars in `check_and_perform_self_install()` and installs itself to `~/.cargo/bin/forge`\\n\\n## Key fixes from aec5f73\\n\\n1. **Env vars set directly**: Used `std::env::set_var()` instead of setting on a Command object (which was ignored by execv())\\n2. **Stable staging path**: Changed from PID-based filename to stable `/tmp/forge-update/forge-staged` so manual restarts work\\n3. **Manual restart detection**: Added fallback to detect stale staging files for users who quit instead of auto-restarting\\n\\n## Retrospective\\n\\n- **What worked:** The atomic staging + exec approach completely avoids the ETXTBSY error. The running binary is never overwritten—instead, we exec a new binary that installs itself on startup.\\n- **What didn't:** The original cargo build + cp approach was fundamentally flawed because it tried to overwrite a running executable. The self-update module had the correct design but had bugs (env var propagation, unstable staging path).\\n- **Surprise:** The `unsafe` block around `env::set_var()` is actually correct here—exec() replaces the entire process image, so no other thread can observe the transient env state.\\n- **Reusable pattern:** For self-updating binaries on Linux: download to staging → exec staging → new binary installs itself on startup. This works because rename() is atomic and the new process isn't \\\"running\\\" from the target location yet.\",\"closed_by_session\":\"\",\"source_system\":\"\",\"source_repo\":\".\",\"deleted_by\":\"\",\"delete_reason\":\"\",\"original_type\":\"\",\"compaction_level\":0,\"original_size\":0,\"sender\":\"\",\"labels\":[\"P0\",\"bug\",\"deferred\",\"failure-count:33\",\"update\"]}\n {\"id\":\"bd-4d2i4\",\"title\":\"Fix test_monthly_costs failing when test runs near month boundary\",\"description\":\"## Problem\\nThe forge-cost integration test `test_monthly_costs` is currently failing with:\\n  left: 2\\n  right: 3\\n\\nThe test inserts 3 API calls: today, 1 day ago, and 2 days ago. When the test runs on May 1 or May 2, one or two of those calls fall in April, outside the current month window. `get_current_month_costs()` only queries the current calendar month.\\n\\n## Fix Options\\n1. Change the test to insert all calls with `now` timestamps (simplest — but loses 'days ago' coverage)\\n2. Change the test to directly call `get_monthly_costs(year, month)` with the year/month of each call and assert individually\\n3. Change the test to use `now - Duration::hours(N)` instead of days to avoid month crossings (hacky)\\n4. Mock the 'current date' or pass timestamps guaranteed to be within one month (best)\\n\\nRecommended: Option 2 — test each period separately, or change calls to use hours within the same day.\\n\\n## Location\\n`crates/forge-cost/tests/integration_tests.rs` line 494\\n\\n## Impact\\nCI fails on `cargo test --workspace` when run near start of month.\",\"design\":\"\",\"acceptance_criteria\":\"\",\"notes\":\"\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":0,\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"assignee\":\"claude-code-glm-4.7-lima\",\"created_at\":\"2026-05-02T19:32:00.698895990Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-05-04T03:20:32.900221459Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-05-04T03:20:32.900221459Z\",\"close_reason\":\"Fixed test_monthly_costs to use hours instead of days for timestamps, ensuring all API calls fall within the same calendar day regardless of when the test runs. This prevents month-boundary failures.\\n\\n## Retrospective\\n- **What worked:** Using Duration::hours instead of days ensures all test data falls within the same calendar day, avoiding month boundary issues entirely. This is a simple, robust fix that works year-round.\\n- **What didn't:** The original Duration::days approach was conceptually correct for testing historical data, but fragile due to month boundaries. Ctrl+U triggers update\\n2. Cargo build succeeds\\n3. cp fails with 'Text file busy'\\n4. Error shown in TUI but update doesn't complete\\n\\n## Investigation\\n\\nSee docs/investigations/fg-2pe-ctrl-u-investigation.md for full analysis.\\n\\n## Recommended Fix\\n\\nThe self_update.rs module already has the correct approach:\\n1. Download new binary to staging location\\n2. On restart, rename staging binary to final location\\n3. Show 'Update ready - restart required' message\\n\\nThe issue is that the current running binary is at ~/.cargo/bin/forge and cannot be overwritten.\\n\\n## Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n- [ ] Update shows clear status during download/build\\n- [ ] Update completes successfully even when forge is running\\n- [ ] User sees 'Update ready - restart required' message\\n- [ ] Restart applies the update correctly\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- docs/investigations/fg-2pe-ctrl-u-investigation.md\\n- crates/forge-core/src/self_update.rs\\n- update-forge.sh\",\"design\":\"\",\"acceptance_criteria\":\"\",\"notes\":\"\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":0,\"issue_type\":\"bug\",\"assignee\":\"claude-code-glm-4.7-echo\",\"owner\":\"\",\"created_at\":\"2026-02-18T05:51:09.766674030Z\",\"created_by\":\"coder\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-28T18:29:40.936641485Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-28T18:29:40.936578096Z\",\"close_reason\":\"## Summary of work completed\\n\\nThe 'Text file busy' error when updating forge has been **fully resolved**. The fix was implemented in commits:\\n- `aec5f73` - Fixed env var propagation and staging path issues\\n- `7ffe04e` - Fixed missing should_quit to trigger restart\\n\\n## How the fix works\\n\\nThe self-update flow now correctly avoids overwriting the running binary:\\n\\n1. **Download to staging**: `perform_update()` downloads the new binary to `/tmp/forge-update/forge-staged` (not the final location)\\n2. **Trigger restart**: After successful download, `should_quit = true` is set, causing the main loop to exit\\n3. **Exec staging binary**: `restart_with_new_binary()` sets env vars (FORGE_AUTO_RESTART, FORGE_INSTALL_PATH, FORGE_STAGING_PATH) and execs the staging binary\\n4. **Self-install on startup**: The new binary detects the restart env vars in `check_and_perform_self_install()` and installs itself to `~/.cargo/bin/forge`\\n\\n## Key fixes from aec5f73\\n\\n1. **Env vars set directly**: Used `std::env::set_var()` instead of setting on a Command object (which was ignored by execv())\\n2. **Stable staging path**: Changed from PID-based filename to stable `/tmp/forge-update/forge-staged` so manual restarts work\\n3. **Manual restart detection**: Added fallback to detect stale staging files for users who quit instead of auto-restarting\\n\\n## Retrospective\\n\\n- **What worked:** The atomic staging + exec approach completely avoids the ETXTBSY error. The running binary is never overwritten—instead, we exec a new binary that installs itself on startup.\\n- **What didn't:** The original cargo build + cp approach was fundamentally flawed because it tried to overwrite a running executable. The self-update module had the correct design but had bugs (env var propagation, unstable staging path).\\n- **Surprise:** The `unsafe` block around `env::set_var()` is actually correct here—exec() replaces the entire process image, so no other thread can observe the transient env state.\\n- **Reusable pattern:** For self-updating binaries on Linux: download to staging → exec staging → new binary installs itself on startup. This works because rename() is atomic and the new process isn't \\\"running\\\" from the target location yet.\",\"closed_by_session\":\"\",\"source_system\":\"\",\"source_repo\":\".\",\"deleted_by\":\"\",\"delete_reason\":\"\",\"original_type\":\"\",\"compaction_level\":0,\"original_size\":0,\"sender\":\"\",\"labels\":[\"P0\",\"bug\",\"deferred\",\"failure-count:33\",\"update\"]}\n {\"id\":\"bd-4d2i4\",\"title\":\"Fix test_monthly_costs failing when test runs near month boundary\",\"description\":\"## Problem\\nThe forge-cost integration test `test_monthly_costs` is currently failing with:\\n  left: 2\\n  right: 3\\n\\nThe test inserts 3 API calls: today, 1 day ago, and 2 days ago. When the test runs on May 1 or May 2, one or two of those calls fall in April, outside the current month window. `get_current_month_costs()` only queries the current calendar month.\\n\\n## Fix Options\\n1. Change the test to insert all calls with `now` timestamps (simplest — but loses 'days ago' coverage)\\n2. Change the test to directly call `get_monthly_costs(year, month)` with the year/month of each call and assert individually\\n3. Change the test to use `now - Duration::hours(N)` instead of days to avoid month crossings (hacky)\\n4. Mock the 'current date' or pass timestamps guaranteed to be within one month (best)\\n\\nRecommended: Option 2 — test each period separately, or change calls to use hours within the same day.\\n\\n## Location\\n`crates/forge-cost/tests/integration_tests.rs` line 494\\n\\n## Impact\\nCI fails on `cargo test --workspace` when run near start of month.\",\"design\":\"\",\"acceptance_criteria\":\"\",\"notes\":\"\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":0,\"issue_type\":\"task\",\"assignee\":\"claude-code-glm-4.7-lima\",\"created_at\":\"2026-05-02T19:32:00.698895990Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-05-04T03:20:32.900221459Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-05-04T03:20:32.900221459Z\",\"close_reason\":\"Fixed test_monthly_costs to use hours instead of days for timestamps, ensuring all API calls fall within the same calendar day regardless of when the test runs. This prevents month-boundary failures.\\n\\n## Retrospective\\n- **What worked:** Using Duration::hours instead of days ensures all test data falls within the same calendar day, avoiding month boundary issues entirely. This is a simple, robust fix that works year-round.\\n- **What didn't:** The original Duration::days approach was conceptually correct for testing historical data, but fragile due to month boundaries.
